programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps Maryam, who is a pet psychic but cannot communicate with her vicious cat Lily. Khloe discovers an entire feral colony behind her parents' house

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy helps families calm their unruly felines, meeting Debbie who is torn between her boyfriend and her incontinent cat Blinkie. Plus, Krisi and Jason want to get married, but standing in their way is her energetic feline Frankie

programme.title: Treehouse Masters

programme.title: The Dark Side of the Sun

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?

programme.title: Fish or Die

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Neil is on the brink of reopening the Motherlode mine for tourism, but a new arrival puts his empire at risk. Jeremy begins a new venture to grow his family farm

programme.title: The Haunted
programme.desc: Family members are horrified when they witness their dog being flung across the room by an unseen entity. Meanwhile, on a nearby farm all the animals are dying. Investigators contemplate whether the cases are related

programme.title: Crikey! It's the Irwins

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff attends a shelter to help the pets of disadvantaged and homeless owners. At the clinic, a dog needs treatment for a growth on its head

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Xena the German shepherd has suffered head injuries after being trampled by a donkey, while Coco the kitten is having trouble walking, so both come in for treatment

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees
programme.desc: A couple from California hopes to adopt a dog that is comfortable in water. Plus, Mariah's mission to save an abandoned dog leads to something extraordinary

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Animal behaviourist Jackson Galaxy visits Ambreen, who has suffered abuse from her cat Calvin for the past 13 years, in a bid to help her get her life back

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Sandy and Phil were happily married until they adopted their cat Woody. Now the couple are convinced that Woody is evil enough to kill their other cat, Pirate

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete creates a treehouse cinema, with loungers elevated into the trees to give a perfect view of the screen and food and drinks served by way of a pulley system

programme.title: Meet the Penguins
programme.desc: The staff of Sancob Seabird Rescue Centre in Cape Town try to help a penguin contaminated by oil. Wannabe dad Winston takes his nest-building too far

programme.title: Meet the Penguins
programme.desc: Crabby, the centre's grumpiest resident, greets a new arrival that melts his icy heart, and Princess - queen of the resident penguins - faces the biggest loss of her young life

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.sub-title: Backwards Birds and Slimy Sea Creatures
programme.desc: High-speed footage reveals how the hummingbird can fly backwards, and a look at the hagfish, which is unlikely to win any underwater beauty contests

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.sub-title: Puppy Dog Tails and Crocodile Jaws
programme.desc: What a dog is saying when it wags its tail, and the secret behind a crocodile's powerful jaws - and how to escape it

programme.title: Little Giants
programme.desc: Bradley and Billy travel to southern California to find a super predator called the sugar glider. These marsupials are the dominant predator for their size

programme.title: Little Giants
programme.desc: The team is in Mpumalanga, South Africa, searching for the Cape mole-rat. The animal has giant, razor-sharp teeth and is known as the 'king of the underground'

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: The spring break-up is starting to occur and the snow is melting. Meanwhile, Neil aims to start construction on the renovation of the mine

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Actor and producer Wilmer Valderrama asks Brett Raymer and Wayde King to build an aquarium with multiple angles and slanted sides

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: A TV producer has trouble feeding the eels in his tank, and the team visits a Malibu winery where a fire caused damage to an outdoor aquarium

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: After a devastating earthquake, Marty saves a log cabin from crumbling to the ground. Meanwhile, Misty replaces a collapsed greenhouse, and Matt constructs a new building

programme.title: Sharks and the City: New York
programme.desc: Biologist Craig O'Connell finds out whether the return of seals to the New York City harbour could also turn around the area's declining great white sharks numbers

programme.title: The Haunted
programme.desc: A couple ignore warnings to leave their home but call in the paranormal team when one of them awakes with a cross painted on their forehead

programme.title: Surviving Joe Exotic
programme.desc: Joe Exotic gives one of his last interviews before his arrest. Plus, ex-employees reveal the truth about his infamous zoo, and there is a look at the rescue of some of its big cats

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Experts answer more questions about the animal kingdom, including whether dogs feel guilty when they do something wrong and why tigers are orange

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Why some cats are cross-eyed, how meerkats are able to eat poisonous scorpions and hippos' ability to sleep while holding their breath

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy gets out of his depth while helping arrange Frank's wedding - and then falls for a bridesmaid on the night before the big day

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on November 19, 1999. Featuring Geri Halliwell, Travis, Ricky Martin, A1, Will Smith, Stereophonics, Prince and Robbie Williams

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on November 26, 1999. Featuring Robbie Williams, Whitney Houston, Glamma Kid, Blur, Cliff Richard, Texas and Wamdue Project

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Bates presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on May 28, 1981. Featuring The Polecats, UB40, Hazel O'Connor, Coast to Coast, Adam & the Ants and Kim Carnes, plus a dance performance from Legs & Co

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: John Peel and Janice Long present this pop chart programme, first broadcast on May 29, 1986. Featuring Doctor and the Medics, Robert Palmer, The Real Thing, Tears for Fears, Pete Wylie, Spitting Image and Peter Gabriel

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs hosts the old-time music hall programme, inviting Frankie Vaughan, Mike and Bernie Winters, Maryetta Midgley, Zena Millar and members of the Players Theatre, London, to entertain an audience at the Leeds City Varieties. First aired in 1977

programme.title: Dusty
programme.desc: Entertainment showcase with host Dusty Springfield and her backing band of Madeline Bell, Lesley Duncan and Maggie Stredder. Dusty's special guests are Alf Garnett - aka actor Warren Mitchell - and actor and writer Ken Campbell. First aired in August 1967

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A victimised German pensioner in a residential home asks Hetty to help trace his long-lost sister. Intent on setting the world to rights, the tenacious sleuth soon finds the elderly gentleman has a few surprises in store. Detective drama, starring Patricia Routledge, Richard Pasco and Suzanne Maddock

programme.title: Ghost Trail (2024)
programme.desc: Hamid joins a secret group tracking Syrian war criminals on the run and his mission takes him to Strasbourg in north-eastern France, pursuing his former torturer for a fateful confrontation. Thriller, starring Adam Bessa. In French and Arabic

programme.title: This Is Joan Collins
programme.desc: The actress reflects on her life and career, looking back on her rollercoaster seven decades in showbusiness and sharing footage from her home archive. Against a backdrop of Collins' own narration, her story showcases the extraordinary life of a woman who has lived through the glitz, the glamour and the enduring moments of Hollywood history, and survived it all with panache

programme.title: Michael Palin and the Mystery of Hammershoi
programme.desc: The actor combines his passion for travel and art as he sets out to discover more about the life and work of Danish artist Vilhelm Hammershoi. Curious to see more of Hammershoi's paintings and discover what kind of life the artist lived, Michael searches for clues in London, the Netherlands and Copenhagen

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs hosts the old-time music hall programme, inviting Frankie Vaughan, Mike and Bernie Winters, Maryetta Midgley, Zena Millar and members of the Players Theatre, London, to entertain an audience at the Leeds City Varieties. First aired in 1977

programme.title: Dusty
programme.desc: Entertainment showcase with host Dusty Springfield and her backing band of Madeline Bell, Lesley Duncan and Maggie Stredder. Dusty's special guests are Alf Garnett - aka actor Warren Mitchell - and actor and writer Ken Campbell. First aired in August 1967

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A victimised German pensioner in a residential home asks Hetty to help trace his long-lost sister. Intent on setting the world to rights, the tenacious sleuth soon finds the elderly gentleman has a few surprises in store. Detective drama, starring Patricia Routledge, Richard Pasco and Suzanne Maddock

programme.title: Wildlife on One
programme.sub-title: African Penguin: Cool Bird in a Hot Spot
programme.desc: The invasion of Cape Town by penguins in 1984 paved the way for others to breed on the African mainland, despite the blazing sunshine and human presence. David Attenborough reveals the interdependence of these birds, which choose a new home each year on the offshore islands or in town

programme.title: Top Hat (1935)
programme.desc: A woman on holiday in London meets a famous dancer staying in the same hotel when his enthusiastic rehearsing wakes her up. Attraction instantly develops between them, but she mistakenly believes he is a producer married to her friend. Musical, starring Fred Astaire, Ginger Rogers, Helen Broderick and Edward Everett Horton, with a score by Irving Berlin, including songs Cheek to Cheek and White Tie and Tails

programme.title: Poems in Their Place
programme.desc: Robert Gittings presents poems by Hardy from Sturminster Newton in Dorset, where the writer enjoyed the only happy years he had with his first wife Emma. First broadcast in 1982

programme.title: Michael Palin and the Mystery of Hammershoi
programme.desc: The actor combines his passion for travel and art as he sets out to discover more about the life and work of Danish artist Vilhelm Hammershoi. Curious to see more of Hammershoi's paintings and discover what kind of life the artist lived, Michael searches for clues in London, the Netherlands and Copenhagen

programme.title: The Magic of Dance with Margot Fonteyn
programme.desc: Margot explores the story of Louis XIV of France and his love of dancing and how the artform evolved from the courtly dances of the 17th-century to what ballet bcame. She heads to the Court Theatre and sees ballet performances, reflecting its original settings and conditions

programme.title: Dance Rebels: A Story of Modern Dance
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the mavericks like Isadora Duncan, Martha Graham, Merce Cunningham and Pina Bausch, whose radical ideas created modern dance in the 20th century. With historical archive and the first-hand experiences of student dancers from the Trinity Laban Conservatoire of Music and Dance, it shows the ideas that challenged audiences and changed dance for ever

programme.title: Nobody's Perfect: The Making of Some Like It Hot
programme.desc: Stars Jack Lemmon and Tony Curtis, together with other members of the cast and crew, recall the making of Some Like It Hot. Featuring rare colour footage from behind the scenes, providing an intimate portrait of Billy Wilder's classic Hollywood comedy

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: How to Write a Mills & Boon
programme.desc: Author Stella Duffy sets out to produce her own novel for Mills & Boon, the company that has become one of the biggest names in romantic fiction. She enlists the help of some avid fans in a challenging mission that takes her from London to Italy and offers an insight into the joy and frustration of writing

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Luke Holder, Dan Mayo-Evans, Anthony Murphy and Ravneet Gill, and special guests Stephen Mangan and Sarah Hadland. Carmen O'Neal picks the drinks

programme.title: Anna Haugh's Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: Anna joins Dennis Taylor in Tyrone at the snooker hall where his career began. She attempts to recreate his grandmother's soda bread recipe. They also join fishermen on the largest lake in the UK to celebrate one of Ireland's favourite delicacies - the Lough Neagh eel

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An update on a new build in Staffordshire as it reaches it conclusion, plus visits to properties in south east London and Greater Manchester. Presented Jacqui Joseph and Dion Dublin

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts from Newark in Nottinghamshire, with experts Ben Cooper and Izzie Balmer helping the teams choose items with profit potential to take to auction. Natasha meets a former Bargain Hunt contestant with a world record collection of bagpipes before having a go herself

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.sub-title: Wigan Warriors v Hull Kingston Rovers 30.05
programme.desc: Wigan Warriors v Hull Kingston Rovers (Kick-off 3.00pm). All the action from the showpiece match at Wembley Stadium, as the last two winners of this trophy battle it out. Rovers are the holders of this competition, having defeated Warrington Wolves 8-6 in the final to lift the trophy for the first time since 1980 and made it back-to-back finals by again beating the Wolves in the last four this time around. Wigan are the most decorated team in the history of this competition with 21 triumphs, and they comfortably defeated St Helens 32-0 in the semi-finals

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the high stakes celebrity quiz. Krishnan Guru-Murthy, the Rev Kate Bottley, Cherry Healey and Babatunde Aleshen must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for the charity of their choice with each correct answer

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the comedy quiz show in which a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win some brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he is joined by Helen George, Ria Lina, Dean McCullough, Josh Widdicombe, Sam Quek and Sam Campbell

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: An evening of entertainment, recorded at the Theatre Royal Drury Lane. TV host AJ Odudu plays Send to All, while Michael surprises an Unexpected Star of the Show with an ambitious indoor skydive prank and takes The Wheel on Wheels to surprise a family somewhere in the UK. Plus, music from Zara Larsson

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Flynn finds proof that Bard has been abusing his men when he is called out to a military exercise where two soldiers have been injured falling into a cavern in the ground. Cam and Indie drop round to visit Alan, only to find he has collapsed, and Dylan tries to try and understand the root cause of his alcoholism. Baby Pearl is finally well enough to leave the hospital, but Faith's anxiety is growing

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: It's Jacob's birthday, and everyone notices there's something up with Zoe. She punishes Dan as she focuses on Solomon's big career break. Dan's romantic trip might offer hope, but it triggers deeply buried memories for Zoe. Jacob is trying to hide his age, not helped by Will's mates gatecrashing their night out

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Champions League
programme.desc: Paris Saint-Germain v Arsenal. Jason Mohammad presents highlights of the final from Puskas Arena in Budapest. The Gunners made it to their first final in this competition since 2006 thanks to a 2-1 semi-final victory over Atlético Madrid, with Bukayo Saka scoring the winner in the second leg at the Emirates. PSG beat Bayern Munich 6-5 across two epic encounters to reach their second successive Champions League final

programme.title: Escape Room (2019)
programme.desc: The prospect of winning a significant sum of money lures a group of individuals into a life-threatening scenario. Being locked in several rooms with extreme conditions, they discover the secrets behind the escape room and must fight to survive by finding a way out. Psychological thriller, starring Deborah Ann Woll, Taylor Russell and Tyler Labine

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Documentary following attempts to find missing people, beginning with police racing to find two teenagers struggling with their mental health. Sixteen-year-old Larissa disappeared after an argument with her mum, while 18-year-old Bobby may be at risk of suicide

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Champions League
programme.desc: Paris Saint-Germain v Arsenal. Jason Mohammad presents highlights of the final from Puskas Arena in Budapest. The Gunners made it to their first final in this competition since 2006 thanks to a 2-1 semi-final victory over Atlético Madrid, with Bukayo Saka scoring the winner in the second leg at the Emirates. PSG beat Bayern Munich 6-5 across two epic encounters to reach their second successive Champions League final

programme.title: Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg
programme.desc: The big names behind the big stories. Laura Kuenssberg talks to those making the news, inside and outside politics.

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents the political developments of the week, with policy-makers questioned on the key issues

programme.title: The Book of Life (2014)
programme.desc: A man who longs to be a musician instead of a bullfighter like his father has to compete with a childhood friend when they fall for the same woman. Their love triangle catches the eye of the spirits of the dead, leading him on a journey through their bizarre world. Animated adventure, with the voices of Diego Luna, Zoe Saldana and Channing Tatum

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts wants to run for the hills due to a tricky legal issue in Devon. Jacqui Joseph is in Eastbourne, East Sussex, where work is already under way at an end-of-terrace. Meanwhile, Dion Dublin heads to Northampton, where a smelly cellar is hard to cope with

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum is in south London meeting Christians who are volunteering their time and talents to help others, ahead of the national Big Help Out and Big Lunch initiatives

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A pair of artists look to Alistair Appleton to help them find an inspiring rural retreat in Somerset where they can further indulge their creativity. Alistair also meets a young couple who moved to the countryside in search of the good life and never looked back, despite ups and downs along the way

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: Extraordinary animal behaviours in the Amazon rainforest, including rare footage of a million turtle hatchlings in southern Brazil making a perilous dash to the river as black caiman prey on them. In Venezuela, a young harpy eagle endures intense storms while in the Pantanal, caiman engage in an elaborate courtship display

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: Wildlife in the north of the American continent, from wolves and polar bears competing for prey in Hudson Bay to male walruses singing together off Alaska's coast. In summer, millions of salmon begin their epic journey upstream, while newborn caribou navigate dangerous river crossings on their first migratory trek. In Alberta, sharp-tailed grouse practise their courtship dances under car headlights

programme.title: Shrek 2 (2004)
programme.desc: The grouchy ogre and his new wife Princess Fiona set out on a journey to meet her parents. However, the king and queen are less than impressed with their daughter's choice of husband, while a conniving fairy godmother is determined to make sure her own son gets to marry the princess. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas, John Cleese, Julie Andrews, Jennifer Saunders and Rupert Everett

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Adam Henson are in Lisburn, Northern Ireland, for the 157th Balmoral Show, the region's largest agricultural and food event, welcoming more than 100,000 visitors each year. Adam explores heritage crafts that the community are determined to preserve and joins families showing their prize livestock, while Sean is in charge of the stopwatch at the Tractor handling competition

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special edition commemorating the 80th anniversary of the D-Day landings, filmed in Normandy, France, and the D-Day Museum Portsmouth. The story of the landings are told through various items brought in by visitors and rare artefacts held in various museums across the UK, and there are eyewitness accounts from those who played a part, including radio operator Marie Scott and veteran Ken Scott

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: A runner is found dead on the set of a Welsh TV drama. John helps Janie to investigate, and is struck by how much of an outsider he feels in what should be his natural home - especially whern he runs into an old co-star. Janie befriends the show's leading actor, leaving John concerned that she will not be able to put her personal connection aside and stay open to all suspects. Guest starring Jim Howick (Ghosts)

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the devastating defeat at Wembley, Southgate's guilt weighs heavily on his conscience. When the fans then turn on the manager, accusing him of prioritising politics over performance, he begins to doubt himself ahead of a controversial World Cup in Qatar. Drama, starring Joseph Fiennes

programme.title: The Nice Guys (2016)
programme.desc: A private detective investigates the murder of a porn star in 1970s Los Angeles, only to run into a hired thug employed to scare him off. However, the mysterious disappearance of a woman connected to the case forces the unlikely duo to work together, leading them to uncover a government conspiracy. Crime comedy, starring Russell Crowe and Ryan Gosling

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Eight famous faces explore their family history, beginning with broadcaster Zoe Ball. With the help of her father, broadcasting favourite Johnny Ball, she uncovers an unexpected connection to Scotland and a tale of hardship and resilience that took the family from industrial 19th-century Glasgow to Bolton. On her late mother's side she hears a tale of mental illness and more migration, as she follows in her ancestors' footsteps from Cornwall to the North East

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It's time for the Mr Teesside Beauty Pageant, and the grand prize is £20,000 - but someone foul lurks in the shadows to attempt to fix the competition

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie has been booked for a drag gig and has invited a load of agents in the hope of making it to the big time, while Mam faces a ghost from her past

programme.title: Paranormal: The Girl, the Ghost and the Gravestone
programme.desc: Sian Eleri meets the current owner of Penyffordd, who bought the farmhouse despite the stories of its haunting. Dr Daniels suggests that moving the gravestone on the property is what started the paranormal activity, leading Sian to investigate if there really is a girl buried in the garden

programme.title: Paranormal: The Girl, the Ghost and the Gravestone
programme.desc: Sian Eleri's investigation into the reported haunting of Penyffordd Farm continues as multiple witnesses claim to see a hooded figure. Could they all have imagined the same thing? As she listens to recordings the original investigator made, Sian discovers the Welsh words for disabled and child had allegedly appeared on the walls. Sian meets with one of the Gower daughters to ask whether at the time she believed that to be the monk apparition focusing in one family member

programme.title: Paranormal: The Girl, the Ghost and the Gravestone
programme.desc: Unable to meet Rose-Mary or David, Sian Eleri visits an exorcist to find out what he believes removing a spirit from the house would entail. She speaks to the daughters of the couple that owned the house at the height of the haunting to question them one last time and Dr Daniels finds a similar case in Spain that might provide a clue as to how the haunting might have been a hoax

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Sports mockumentary about GB Paralympian Darren Silverstone, who stole the hearts of a nation before his infamous chokey-o in Tokyo. For the first time, he tells his story - and starts his 'glorious comeback'

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It's time for the Mr Teesside Beauty Pageant, and the grand prize is £20,000 - but someone foul lurks in the shadows to attempt to fix the competition

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie has been booked for a drag gig and has invited a load of agents in the hope of making it to the big time, while Mam faces a ghost from her past

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor and Clara arrive in the near future on a run-down space shuttle heading for the moon, with one of Clara's pupils along for the ride. Crash-landing on the surface, they are confronted with a mining base full of corpses, vicious, spider-like creatures waiting in the dark, and a terrible decision for the human race that leaves Clara wondering if she knows the Doctor at all

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor takes Clara for a ride aboard a future version of the Orient Express travelling through space. The passengers and crew are being stalked by a creature from ancient legend that is invisible to everyone except its intended prey - who only have 66 seconds to stop it from killing them. Guest starring Frank Skinner

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Things look up for Miranda's love life after she finds herself with two dates - one with the best friend of Tilly's fiance, the other with a charming acquaintance of Stevie. She is especially thrilled by her rendezvous with the latter - but problems arise when she attracts even more attention from an unwanted source. Comedy, starring Miranda Hart

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Stevie and Miranda compete for the attention of waitress Tamara, hoping to prove they are better friend material, but soon realise that trying to keep up with a twentysomething is surprisingly tiring. The duo are offended when their new acquaintance assumes they are in their forties, and set about proving their youthfulness

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The doting mother offers Winnie and Sharon a place to stay when the pipes in their house burst, but Agnes and Cathy soon discover that living with best friends isn't as much fun as they thought it would be. As if that wasn't enough for Agnes to cope with, she is asked by Father Damian to do a eulogy for a neighbour she never knew and there's Bono's 13th birthday party to host as well

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes discovers that Peggy Piper, the Pernod poisoner, is being released from prison and could be coming back to town - which is bad news for Winnie as she was the one who saw her old friend poison her husband and gave the evidence that put her behind bars in the first place. Meanwhile, Dermot hires an expensive tank costume for his latest promotion - only for Buster to go and lose it. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll, Dermot O'Neill, Jennifer Gibney, Gary Hollywood and guest starring Sue Vincent

programme.title: Are You There God? It's Me, Margaret (2023)
programme.desc: When her family moves home, 11-year-old Margaret is uprooted from her life in New York City for the suburbs of New Jersey, going through the throes of puberty with new friends in a new school. She relies on her mother, who is also struggling to adjust to life outside the big city, and her adoring grandmother, who isn't happy they moved away.Family drama based on Judy Blume's bestseller, starring Abby Ryder Fortson, Rachel McAdams and Kathy Bates

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Invited into the traditionally private gypsy community, Stacey discovers the complex balancing act young women face growing up in one of Britain's most maligned ethnic minorities. In Bedfordshire she meets 23-year-old Chantelle, who prides herself in keeping with the 'old' gypsy values her grandmother taught her and shares them on social media, where she has nearly 400,000 TikTok followers. In Lincolnshire, the reporter meets 18-year-old Serena, who is planning her wedding to her fiancé Marco. He is also from a gypsy and traveller community and Serena wouldn't have it any other way

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Gaz decides the time has come to propose to Janet. Scared she is losing her friends, Donna offers to help - although Tim believes she is holding on to the past. Elsewhere, Janet is rushed to hospital after experiencing stomach pains while helping Wesley on his market stall, but when they try to contact Gaz he is nowhere to be found

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet returns from hospital and tells Gaz she spent the night with Wesley holding her hand, prompting him to confess he slept with Donna. After being thrown out of the house, Gaz realises he still loves his former wife and feels torn between the two women. Meanwhile, Louise is desperate for a boyfriend

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Short comedy film starring Luke Rollason. A social media meltdown forces Geoffrey to reassses his life and values

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor and Clara arrive in the near future on a run-down space shuttle heading for the moon, with one of Clara's pupils along for the ride. Crash-landing on the surface, they are confronted with a mining base full of corpses, vicious, spider-like creatures waiting in the dark, and a terrible decision for the human race that leaves Clara wondering if she knows the Doctor at all

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor takes Clara for a ride aboard a future version of the Orient Express travelling through space. The passengers and crew are being stalked by a creature from ancient legend that is invisible to everyone except its intended prey - who only have 66 seconds to stop it from killing them. Guest starring Frank Skinner

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max confronts the Mitchells about his suspicions, Gina gives Eddie the cold shoulder, and Zack gets some unwelcome news

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant steps up to protect his family, Zack purposely raises suspicions, while Eddie and Harry clash

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It is the day of Vicki and Ross's wedding, but it remains to be seen whether it goes ahead, and Priya gets a shock

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chaos erupts at Vicki and Ross's wedding, and Denise receives some news

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Emma Myers returns as teenage amateur sleuth Pip Fitz-Amobi. Pip is determined to stay away from any more investigations and focus on her friends, school and Ravi. But when a key witness in Max Hastings' trial disappears, Pip feels the pull of a new case. Adapted by Holly Jackson from her novel Good Girl Bad Blood, and co-starring Henry Ashton, Zain Iqbal and Jude Morgan-Collie

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Connor becomes increasingly anxious about his brother's disappearance and begs Pip to help him find Jamie. Despite her reluctance, she eventually realises she has no choice and is adamant Max must be involved somehow. But when she seemingly hits a dead end, she is forced to go back to the drawing board and retrace Jamie's last steps

programme.title: Arthur the King (2024)
programme.desc: A professional adventure racer looking for one last chance at glory joins a team of athletes on a 435-mile, 10-day event in the Dominican Republic, with the aid of a scrappy stray dog. Fact-based drama, starring Mark Wahlberg, Simu Liu, Juliet Rylance and Nathalie Emmanuel

programme.title: Deepfake Porn: Could You Be Next?
programme.desc: The rising trend of harmless images being turned into hardcore porn, footage that is often used against the people depicted with devastating consequences. The film features three women who have been impacted by this form of image-based sexual abuse, including a leading Florida State Senator, Lauren Book, the UK campaigner Kate Isaacs, and Dina, a woman who was deep-faked by a work colleague

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: In Britain shoplifting is at an all-time high, incidents of violence and abuse against shop staff have skyrocketed, and a third of Brits have admitted to stealing through the self-checkouts. To understand this apparent high-street epidemic, Stacey heads to Manchester to meet the people behind the crime and spends time on the shop floor with the staff trying to prevent further thefts

programme.title: Just Act Normal
programme.desc: When their mother disappears, Tiana, Tionne and Tanika fight to hide the truth from the outside world in order to stay together as a family. As Tiana struggles to keep their regular lives afloat, Tionne displaces his grief and Tanika tries to convince her teacher to adopt them, chaos erupts when their mum's hapless drug dealer, Dr Feelgood, moves in. Comedy drama, starring Chenée Taylor, Kaydrah Walker-Wilkie and Akins Subair

programme.title: Just Act Normal
programme.desc: The siblings' secret is exposed. Tiana plans to escape but fails and is forced to confront her estranged dad. Feelgood offers to help, but there's a catch - he wants to move in. Comedy drama, starring Chenée Taylor, Kaydrah Walker-Wilkie and Akins Subair

programme.title: The Trials of Pa Salieu
programme.desc: Documentary following the British-Gambian musician as he attempts to rebuild his life and career after prison, returning to live performance while still on probation, navigating curfews, travel restrictions and the risk of recall. He makes his comeback at the Glastonbury Festival, but touring in Europe hangs in the balance, and he faces a choice between breaking commitments and risking a return to prison

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: A desolate train track seems the perfect spot to end it all - until someone else turns up with the same idea. An awkward situation ensues. Comedy, with Seroca Davis and Joe Wilkinson

programme.title: Everything Went Fine (2021)
programme.desc: When André, 85, has a stroke, his daughter Emmanuèle hurries to her father's bedside. Sick and half-paralysed in his hospital bed, he asks her to help him end his life. Adaptation of Emmanuèle Bernheim's memoir, starring Sophie Marceau and André Dussollier. In French

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and tour guide Paul Gorton test Nitin Ganatra, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Gladiator Apollo, on their knowledge of the West Midlands town of Dudley

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: On an ill-fated group boat trip, Zoe tries to pretend last night's betrayal didn't mean anything. But Dan's behaviour threatens to push her over the edge. Nat uses the kiss to drive a wedge between Jacob and his boyfriend Will, who has shown up uninvited. Solomon is the only one who doesn't know what happened last night - does Zoe tell him and destroy two marriages? Jess is forced to take drastic action to avoid confrontation

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The Piris face a few tricky problems, but they discover that swapping items and working together can provide clever solutions, turning a regular day into an extraordinary one

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: When DORIS's satnav breaks, Big Lizard teaches Cosima how to find her way

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy promises to go camping with the ducklings, but he wants to watch football instead. During half-time, Weasy attempts to finish camping as quickly as possible

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Twinkle feels she is being taken for granted by the other heroes, so she joins the Fairy Squad when they arrive

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy struggles to pick coconuts, but the Lemmings find a magic conch

programme.title: Duck & Frog
programme.desc: When Duck gets a job as a baker, Frog realises he can get free treats, but a mix-up with the dough causes a birthday cake to grow out of control

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: Trainers put an intriguing new device into action at an Exceed testing centre

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Adam and Joe become bus tour guides, except they must say and do as the other says. With Holly H and Radio 1's Rickie joining as passengers, what could go wrong?

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.sub-title: Diamond, Cyclone, Nitro, Fire v the Restaurant Rematch!
programme.desc: Diamond, Cyclone, Nitro and Fire go head-to-head dishing out the dares in a restaurant. Will a robot, rockstar, broccoli or water over the head cause a tap out?

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.sub-title: Radio 1's Big Weekend Competition Winner Revealed!
programme.desc: The team is with the lucky competition winner at Radio 1's Big Weekend, where Joel, Abby and Shini try DIY festival make-up looks. The Adventures of Rap Kid by MC Grammar is the latest read in the Blue Peter Book Club

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don is bringing bold colour to the garden, planting gingers and cannas, setting out tomatoes and sowing wallflowers straight into the soil. From her Devon garden, Carol Klein explores what is really going on inside a seed from the moment it is sown. Rekha Mistry is also on a mission to grow big in a small space, using only pots on her patio

programme.title: Jailhouse Rock (1957)
programme.desc: A petty hoodlum imprisoned for manslaughter is taught how to play the guitar by his cellmate and becomes an international rock 'n' roll star. Musical drama, starring Elvis Presley, Dean Jones and Mickey Shaughnessy

programme.title: Live Women's Challenge Cup
programme.desc: Wigan Warriors v St Helens (Kick-off 11.45pm). All the action from the showpiece match at Wembley Stadium. These teams also met in last season's final, with Wigan claiming a comfortable 42-6 victory. The holders were in fine form again in this term's semi-finals, thrashing York Valkyrie 52-0, but Saints also impressed in a 30-14 win against Leeds Rhinos

programme.title: Nicholas and Alexandra (1971)
programme.desc: Epic historical costume drama chronicling the reign of Nicholas II, the last tsar of Russia, until his overthrow in the 1917 October Revolution and exile in Siberia with his family, culminating in their assassination. Starring Michael Jayston, Janet Suzman, Laurence Olivier, Jack Hawkins, Tom Baker, Harry Andrews and Michael Redgrave

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The lives of white gyrfalcons and a pack of Arctic wolves on the frozen landscape of Ellesmere Island, Canada. The birds feed their young with the flesh of hares, while the lupine pack awaits the arrival of new cubs. Narrated by Simon Poland

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Presenter Paul Martin and antiques experts Adam Partridge and Will Axon visit Ickworth House, an Italianate palace near Bury St Edmunds in Suffolk. Among the items valued are a chinoiserie travelling clock found at the back of a wardrobe and a campaign writing box that raises the roof when it goes to auction. Plus, Paul meets the people trying to safeguard the future of the Suffolk Punch horse, which holds a unique place in the history of the county

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: From a windswept island chapel battered by the North Sea to a quiet Welsh farmhouse that shaped the future of a language, this episode travels to two remote National Trust sites whose powerful stories have inspired centuries of pilgrims - and reveals the delicate conservation work protecting their legacy. Toby Jones narrates

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Part two of two. The historian explores how the battle for American independence was not fought only on American soil, but also unfolded in Britain itself, with sabotage, espionage, and political unrest reshaping the empire. She reflects on a revolution that created an independent America and also reshaped Britain and its monarch

programme.title: Live Boxing
programme.desc: Adam Azim v Steve Claggett. Coverage of the super lightweight bout at Wembley Arena. Azim is undefeated in his 14 professional fights to date, with his last contest coming in November, when he stopped Kurt Scoby in the 12th round. However, Canadian Claggett is a seasoned campaigner, who has bounced back with two early stoppages against Eduardo Estela and Alejandro Frias Rodriguez after a failed challenge for Teofimo Lopez's world title in 2024

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A selection of memorable Top of the Pops performances from the year. Dance music and Europop continued to dominate the charts with performances from Haddaway, Sub Sub, Ace of Base and Robin S, while the early stages of Britpop are represented by Suede and Radiohead. Icelandic superstar Björk makes an early appearance and there are the big sellers by Meat Loaf and Gabrielle and hits by Jamiroquai and Chaka Demus & Pliers

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Top of the Pops performances from the year, including appearances by Blur, Corona, Tori Amos, PJ and Duncan, Sheryl Crow, East 17, Whigfield, the Cranberries, Dawn Penn, Elastica, Wet Wet Wet, Shampoo, Elton John, Pretenders, Kylie Minogue, Youssou Ndour featuring Neneh Cherry, the Grid, Kate Bush, Bon Jovi, Crash Test Dummies and D: Ream

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce hosts the topical debate in Dulwich, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: A food shack in a fishing village is burned down with its owner inside. John takes a holiday in the village to recover from recent troubles, but cannot resist a mystery. Janie struggles to balance her new responsibilities with the detective she wants to be as they uncover the connections between fierce business rivalries, a waning fishing industry and a rare breeding seal colony

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: Fact-based drama, starring Joseph Fiennes as Gareth Southgate, exploring the England manager's innovative methods to turn around the team's fortunes. Southgate takes on a team at an all-time low and a jaded fanbase after an embarrassing defeat to Iceland. Haunted by his own disastrous penalty miss as a player, he enlists the aid of a psychologist ( Jodie Whittaker) to change the team's mindset and tackle their biggest fear

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: South Wales: A Critical Season in the Forest
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Sammi Kinghorn explore the forests of the Welsh valleys at a critical time. with wildfires posing a growing threat as spring gives way to summer. They meet people shaping the future of these landscapes, from fire prevention and recreation to conservation and training the next generation. Meanwhile, Tom Heap heads to Devon and Cornwall to investigate the state of the UK's seaweed farming industry

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Ruth Vichos tries producing a mushroom crop from a kit and there's a report on some of Scotland's best bonsai with everything viewers need to know to start that form of cultivation. Brian Cunningham is in the seaside garden at Beechgrove refreshing the planting with varieties that can withstand a coastal climate and also turns conifers into sculptures with topiary. Plus, the week's handy hints

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits Bee Edge farm near Eyemouth in Berwickshire to help open the barn doors and let the cows out for summer. Continuing to mark Landward's 50th anniversary, Cammy Wilson catches up with a farming pioneer, while Rosie Morton steps back in time in Aberdeenshire to finding outwhat an old farm building can reveal about the nation's agriculrural past. Leanna Fernandez is on the trail of a walrus that has been turning up in harbours around the north coast

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents more highlights, with recipes from Imad Alarnab, Shivi Ramoutar, Patrick Williams and Ravneet Gill, while comedian Laura Smyth faces her food heaven or hell

programme.title: Great Manchester Run
programme.desc: Live coverage of the 10k and half-marathon races, as athletes of all abilities run through the city's streets. Ethiopians Selemon Barega and Medina Eisa won the respective men's and women's 10k races here last year, and the honours went to Brits Josh Birmingham and Melissa Duncan in the half marathon

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: The food writer prepares simple, tasty meals from ingredients that are often overlooked, including a lentil and chard stew

programme.title: Mary Berry - Cook & Share
programme.desc: Mary journeys to some of the places closest to her heart, sharing memories from her past and creating new ones, while inspiring those she meets along the way with delicious recipes. Here Mary works behind the scenes at an Indian wedding, helping the caterers pull-off a huge event that feeds 400 people from morning to night, and adding a recipe of her own to the menu. Blown away by the traditions, customs and vibrancy of the wedding, Mary takes time to reflect on her own special day

programme.title: Operation Crossbow (1965)
programme.desc: Three Allied engineers parachute into occupied Holland on a mission to infiltrate and destroy a German missile base. The trio team up with the local resistance, but two of the men are unaware the third is a Nazi agent. Second World War adventure, starring George Peppard, Sophia Loren, Trevor Howard, Tom Courtenay and John Mills

programme.title: The Searchers (1956)
programme.desc: An embittered American Civil War veteran sets out to find his niece, who has been abducted by a renegade Comanche tribe. His search takes him many years, and during the long quest his travelling companion starts to wonder if he intends to rescue her or kill her. John Ford's Western, starring John Wayne, Natalie Wood, Jeffrey Hunter and Vera Miles

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and the team head to the Tian Shan mountains of Kyrgyzstan in search of snow leopards in the remote Djangart valley Joining forces with Kyrgyz conservation group Snow Leopard Foundation, Steve and the team set out to find evidence of a breeding population of snow leopards in the hope of persuading the government of Kyrgyzstan to turn the valley into a protected nature reserve

programme.title: Southbank at 75: You Are Here
programme.desc: An epic dance spectacular to mark London's Southbank Centre's anniversary, forming an electrifying journey through 75 years of British youth culture and featuring the music of Shirley Bassey, the Verve, Bronski Beat and EsDeeKid among others. A freewheeling journey of discovery across decades, remixing and reimagining some of the most vivid and influential youth and social movements that have driven culture forward since 1951 when the south bank of the Thames hosted the Festival of Britain. Rebellion meets euphoria as a cast of hundreds take viewers on a trip through northern soul, dancehall and disco, and from Teddy Boys to the heady intensity of a nightclub dancefloor

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: The Meeting International Mohammed VI d'Athlétisme de Rabat. Coverage of the third round of the series, held at Rabat Olympic Stadium in Morocco. Dutch star Femke Bol produced a fine display in the 400m hurdles here last year, winning in a meeting record time of 52.46 seconds. There were also meeting records for Botswana's Tshepiso Masalela and American Jonah Koech in the men's 800m and 1500m respectively

programme.title: The Mother of All Cons
programme.desc: Documentary examining the story behind Believe in Magic, a charity for seriously ill children that was launched in 2010 by 16-year-old Megan Bhari, whose mother claimed she had been diagnosed with a brain tumour three years before. Inspired by Megan's courage and self-sacrifice, celebrities and her beloved One Direction were more than willing to donate their time and money to the cause and raised money to send Meghan abroad for expensive treatment in America. But in an online chatroom, some parents whose own children have been treated for cancer abroad began asking questions

programme.title: Later... with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Taking to the stage at Alexandra Palace Theatre in north London are singer-songwriters Jessie Ware and Holly Humberstone, soul singer Samm Henshaw, electronic band Fcukers, and producer, songwriter and musician Jack Antonoff. Music video director Tim Pope chats to Jools at the piano about his forthcoming book I Shoot Rock Stars

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents the political developments of the week, with policy-makers questioned on the key issues

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Surgeons drain all of their patient's blood from his body to replace damaged arteries. A patient's fertility is at risk without a complex operation to remove diseased bowel

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the wildlife of the Dee valley, from wintering birds along the estuary, to displaying black grouse on moorland, rare freshwater-pearl mussels, and spawning sea trout

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: South Wales: A Critical Season in the Forest
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Sammi Kinghorn explore the forests of the Welsh valleys at a critical time. with wildfires posing a growing threat as spring gives way to summer. They meet people shaping the future of these landscapes, from fire prevention and recreation to conservation and training the next generation. Meanwhile, Tom Heap heads to Devon and Cornwall to investigate the state of the UK's seaweed farming industry

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve joins forces with ropes expert Aldo Kane and Saudi Arabian geologists Mahmoud Al-Shanti and Hala Alwagdani to explore a volcanic underworld in search of the longest lava tube in the Arabian Peninsula. Geological evidence suggests that at least 400km of tunnels lie beneath the Harrat Khaybar lava field, subterranean passageways that could contain bones and artefacts from ancient civilisations. Only 25km of these lava tubes have ever been explored - until now

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: Niall and Ava face major life changes as Ruben struggles with his relationship. An impulsive choice between Mona and Niall has serious consequences for all involved. Hard-hitting drama, written by and starring Richard Gadd, with Jamie Bell, Amy Manson and Anjli Mohindra

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: A breakthrough World Cup reignites belief, and the England team return to a tournament on home turf, amidst the Covid pandemic. As the pressure to win increases, Pippa Grange questions her role. Meanwhile, Gareth tries to unite a divided country ahead of Euro 2020, where he hopes the team finally bring it home at Wembley

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Gary Pe assist the teams at an antiques centre in Hele near Exeter, and Christina Trevanion visits a Devon pub frozen in time, where everything has been left untouched since the 1960s

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Before boarding his train at Budapest's historic Nyugati station, Michael Portillo explores its sumptuous royal waiting room, built specially for the Austro-Hungarian emperor Franz Joseph I and his empress, Elizabeth. Heading south, his next destination is the Hungarian of Szeged, close to the Serbian and Romanian borders

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Guest judge Fred Sirieix joins the team for the penultimate round, to pick one last candidate for the final. The judges visit two and a half acres of mixed gardens in Fintona with flower beds, a vegetable garden, and an apple orchard, a formal Italianate area and large woodland restored from once-barren farmland in Omagh, and a romantic rose garden overlooking Dundrum Bay

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball explains how it isn't necessary to stop eating chocolate to improve health - as long as it's dark chocolate. She travels to Birmingham where she shows fitness fanatic Bilal how chocolate with at least 70 per cent cocoa could boost heart health, improve brain function and even reduce the risk of type 2 diabetes

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.desc: Andy McGrath visits one of Edinburgh's eerie vaults and encounters an inexplicable ghostly phenomenon. He retraces the steps of notorious serial killers Burke and Hare, explores the story of Felicity the puma, the only big cat ever captured in Britain, and discovers the decaying remains of the trap that once captured her, hidden in an overgrown forest. Later, he travels to the battlefield of Culloden to highlight how this significant battle profoundly impacted Scotland's future before heading to Lochs Morar and Ness to test his remote submersible in the depths of these infamous monster lakes

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence
programme.desc: Evidence and modern-day sightings cause experts to explore links between Native American myths and UFOs

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Crocodile Monster, Tiktok USO and Ghost Dog Collar Removal
programme.desc: The team investigates legendary man-eating croc Gustav, glowing lights in Mexico and chilling footage of a pair of dogs acting like they have just seen a ghost

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly
programme.desc: Enthusiasts scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's automotive junk and classic collectibles

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Kane Hodder, an actor who played Jason Voorhees in four of the Friday the 13th films, comes to American Jewelry to have a gold pendant featuring his trademark hockey goalie mask

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les and Ashley must properly determine the value of a shoddy bike. Meanwhile, Seth enters into a conflict with a New Jersey man, and a customer desires a TV

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Seth plots to empty the overflowing warehouse behind Les's back, but risks upsetting his father

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson travels to Loughborough, Leicestershire, to help members of the public decide whether to put their treasures up for auction, or accept a cash sum from one of a team of antiques experts. Simon Schneider is tempted by a striking cigar box, while Mark Stevens is forced to listen to some harsh words

programme.title: Storage Wars: Texas
programme.desc: Bubba, Kenny and Matt compete fiercely over the bargains on offer at an auction in Forth Worth

programme.title: Storage Wars: Texas
programme.desc: The bidders go to Waxahachie, where Jenny makes a scandalous discovery that could prove very profitable

programme.title: Storage Wars: Texas
programme.desc: While Kenny and Matt bicker over a tool-rich locker in DeSoto, Mary bags a purchase that sparks a trip to the oldest Italian restaurant in Texas

programme.title: Storage Wars: Texas

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: Genius is widely considered to be the ability to change the world in an incredible way, like inventing new technology, deciphering the mysteries of the universe, or creating transcendent art, but its origin is a mystery

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: More than 80 per cent of the Earth's oceans remain unexplored, and in many ways, the underwater world is the host's 'final frontier'. As new technology allows people to explore oceans and deep lakes more than ever before, nobody knows what they will find

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: According to folklore, pots of gold and wishes are granted if a Leprechaun is caught. These mysterious little creatures are seemingly only found in Ireland. Alleged sightings of leprechauns and fairies are nothing new in Ireland. In fact, many claim they are part of everyday life on the Emerald Isle. Experts debate whether these Celtic creatures are fact or fiction

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: The mysterious phone call from an apparently middle-aged woman who predicted the murder of President John F Kennedy. On November 22 1963, a telephone operator in California answered a call that stated that the President was going to die. This came some hours before Lee Harvey Oswald unleashed his fire

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: God moves in mysterious ways and sometimes appears in mysterious places. This episode lools at claims of mysterious religious sightings. Why do so many people see a vision of Jesus Christ in seemingly normal sights such as a tree stump or an antique cabinet?

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Hook Island Monster, Skydivers' Plane Stall, and Somalia Freighter Phantom
programme.desc: The team explores possible proof of a sea monster and a tale of a skydive gone wrong. Plus, a spectral image recorded on a Freighter ship in Somalia

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Panama UFO Disc, Bloody Red Sea and the Dyatlov Pass Incident
programme.desc: An examination of several seemingly impossible things 'caught on film', including a flying saucer-shaped object spotted near the Panama Canal and a 'bloody' Red Sea in Egypt. Plus, claims a group of hikers were brutally killed by a Yeti in the mountains of Russia

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Crocodile Monster, Tiktok USO and Ghost Dog Collar Removal
programme.desc: The team investigates legendary man-eating croc Gustav, glowing lights in Mexico and chilling footage of a pair of dogs acting like they have just seen a ghost

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: A giant carving of a strange creature appears in the Australian Outback, and strange bright green lights illuminate the seas around Thailand

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: A buried monument offers new evidence about a huge Biblical mystery. A giant shadow engulfs the Pacific Ocean, and strange circular lake is home to unearthly life

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: A sinister giant swastika that appeared in a forest in Germany. Plus, mysterious markings spotted in an isolated corner of Antarctica - how did they get there?

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost Liners
programme.desc: The design, service and loss of the Achille Lauro, a ship which suffered two serious collisions, multiple onboard fires and was even hijacked by PLO terrorists in 1985

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost Liners
programme.desc: Documentary charting the fate of the MTS Oceanos, a French-built and Greek-owned cruise ship that sank off the coast of South Africa in 1991 after suffering uncontrolled flooding to its engine room

programme.title: Hazardous History with Henry Winkler
programme.desc: The modern concept of extreme sports is a product of marketing, but in the past a lack of rules made them truly dangerous. The actor highlights some examples, from bone-crushing football and deadly baseball games to wild events like auto polo

programme.title: Hitler's Handmaidens
programme.desc: The Second World War unleashed the full horror of the Nazis on Poland, the Baltic States, Ukraine and Russia. Approximately 500,000 German women served on the Eastern front and the settlement of conquered lands commenced. The colonisers' death camps were established with support by secretaries, administrators, SS wives and female concentration camp guards. This episode explores how women were active participants and perpetrators in the war and its attendant atrocities - especially the Holocaust

programme.title: Hitler's Secret Sex Life
programme.desc: The myriad of rumours, theories and disputed historical accounts of Adolf Hitler's sexual psychology, considering how it integrated into his overall twisted psyche. This series addresses various time periods of Hitler's alleged and proven sex life and explores the role sex played in shaping Hitler's behaviour

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.desc: In Staffordshire, Andy McGrath joins forces with Dr Kate Cherrell to explore the legends of the Kidsgrove boggart and a haunted canal. He seeks the lair of Sir Gawain's enemy, the Green Knight, while uncovering tales of werewolves, black-eyed children, and trolls in Cannock Chase

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Documentary exploring electric blue mountain and the gateway to Hell, the railroad of death and the town-swallowing Texan abyss

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There

programme.desc: Tony Harris and his team look at whether ISS images signify an imminent alien invasion and whether kayakers in Washington encountered a lake monster - or something even worse. Plus, the possibility that rats are joining forces to create a super rat collective

programme.title: Evidence of the Unexplained

programme.sub-title: Unlocking the Secrets of the Nazca Lines
programme.desc: Laurence Fishburne explores the Nazca Lines. Dubbed 'the world's greatest archaeological enigma' these 2,000-year-old geoglyphs span over 50 miles of the Peruvian desert. The drawings are so massive they can only be seen from the air. Fishburne contemplates whether new research might finally unravel their secrets

programme.title: Medieval Murder Mysteries
programme.sub-title: King Edward II: A Mysterious Death
programme.desc: In England, 1327, the body of the recently disposed king Edward II lays on his cell at Berkeley Castle, waiting to be embalmed. If official accounts are to be believed, Edward died from natural causes while in custody after his forced abdication. For some, the demise of the former king was suspiciously timely and all too convenient for his enemies

programme.title: What on Earth?

programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the story of Stewart Britain by visiting Stirling, the seat of the Scottish royal family for 200 years. She joins a team of archaeologists in uncovering remnants of the docks, explores the secret passageways of Stirling Castle, learns how to make medieval ale, and discovers the origins of football

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on an expedition along Dorset's Jurassic coast to explore the county's hidden Second World War story. Starting beside the defences on Chesil Beach, he heads east to finish in Swanage and Studland Bay. On his five-day walk he encounters stunning scenery and uncovers wartime acts of ingenuity and bravery. He also discovers the crucial part a swannery played in developing a secret weapon and reveals the role of Dorset in protecting Britain from invasion during the conflict

programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a festive episode in which celebrity contestants John Inverdale, Judy Murray, Bernard Cribbins and Scarlett Moffatt pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Chase

They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught
