programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: This week's campers - Simon and Emmy, Sean and Marie, and Jen, Ian and Ellis - arrive at Blair Castle Caravan Park at the edge of the Cairngorms National Park

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Shannon Slater-Dent and Trevor Tredgett's Bove Town House in Glastonbury. The duo are keen to show off what their B&B is all about, so lead an afternoon of group sound healing. While Ben Davenport and Sian Dewaal from the Fritton Arms immediately recognise that this B&B will be tough competition, Stevie King and his head chef Dave from SugarBeat Eating House dismiss the activity as being 'hippy'. However, it's the poor night's sleep that bothers Kathryn and her best friend Trevor, from Treberthan B&B, as well as the fact they have to cook their own breakfast. At feedback time, the idea of self B&B comes under fire and Kathryn doesn't hold back with her sleep score

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Treberthan B&B in Par, Cornwall, where owner Kathryn Langley and her best friend Trevor Burnell set out to give their guests a taste of Cornish hospitality. Everyone's impressed by the high standard of cleanliness, but Stevie and David are put off by the old-fashioned decor. A Cornish pasty making competition reveals Kathryn's assertive streak, while professional chef David feels under pressure to win, Ben and Sian make an unconventional pasty, and Shannon and Trevor forget a vital ingredient. At breakfast, a request for goat's milk puts Kathryn under pressure

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Stevie King and his head chef David Lovett host at SugarBeat Eating House in Swainsthorpe in Norfolk. The cleanliness comes under fire as Kathryn and Trevor struggle with their marked shower screen, and Ben and Sian write a message in the dust they find. Over dinner, Ben goes into detective mode to find out everything he can about his SugarBeat rivals. However, it's Kathryn's straight talking that becomes the talking point for Shannon and Trevor

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Fritton Arms in Norfolk, run by Ben Davenport and partner Sian Dewaal. David and Stevie are upset about their feedback so they conduct a forensic-style room inspection - complete with rubber gloves - and a paddleboat race on a nearby lake brings out everyone's competitive nature. Ben impresses the guests by revealing the hotel's aristocratic connections - but soon comes under fire for allowing pets to stay

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners reunite to find out what they have been paid, with Shannon and Trevor from Bove Town House wanting to discuss Kathryn's comments about her poor night's sleep

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson helps Gary and Jan find their dream holiday home in southwest France. programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson goes house-hunting with retired NHS theatre nurse Linda and painter and decorator Peter around the countryside of Abruzzo in Italy. programme.title: A Place in the Sun

They call on Jasmine Harman to help them find a detached house with a pool for their budget of £250,000

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In north Surrey, planning enforcement officer Becky is joined by singing teacher Petrina, drum teacher Rob, solicitor Shoba and building company boss Giovanni. Becky plans to delight her guests with an evening of modern home-cooking and a menu including butternut, lamb and a deconstructed apple dessert. The week gets off to an intriguing start with young gun Rob taking a shine to Petrina and vice versa

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Solicitor Shoba hosts the second night in north Surrey, and tries to impress with an Indian-themed menu. However, the guests are less than impressed with the food, especially the main course - a curry made from frozen fish - but will a bit of belly dancing, Bollywood-style, help save the day? Meanwhile, singer Petrina and drummer Rob continue their flirting both at and away from the table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Drum tutor Rob hosts the third night in north Surrey, hoping his Thai-themed menu will impress his guests, as well as the object of his affections, Petrina. He serves prawns with lemon, lime and chilli for his starter, Thai chicken curry for his main, and rounds off with his own take on an ice-cream sundae. As a single man living in his stepmother's house, Rob thinks his competitors will see him as an underdog, so is intent on pulling out all the stops

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Singing teacher Petrina hosts the fourth dinner party in north Surrey, and she believes her experience of entertaining on cruise ships will give her the edge as she plans a showstopper of an evening. However, the diners seem more interested in their host's continued flirting with fellow guest Rob. Will tonight be the night, or will he shy away when he discovers Petrina has sung in the West End and worked with dozens of household names?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Giovanni hosts the final night of the competition in north Surrey, intent on flying the flag for Sicily with some family inspired recipes. Even with help from his father, a tagliatelle tragedy leaves him with Parmesan on his face. Becky is less than impressed with Giovanni's dishes, and matters go from bad to worse when he attempts to serve his home-made pasta. However, poor food doesn't deter a romantic Rob from popping the question to Petrina, sending shockwaves across the dining room. In a final attempt to bag the £1,000, Giovanni invites an opera singer to liven up the proceedings, but his musical initiative may prove too little to save the day

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A woman is transferred to St George's after crashing her car into a tree, and has to have her broken ankle very quickly realigned if she is to have any hope of walking again. programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

An elderly man falls over and badly cuts his head. He lives alone with help from carers and is fiercely independent, but his son is concerned this will happen again

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Guildford, Surrey, the first host is glamorous 22-year-old cosmetic-surgery-fan Shanice, who hopes to show she is more than just a pretty face by serving up sumptuous food and dazzling conversation. The guests she must impress are 32-year-old entertainer David, 69-year-old Norwegian plain-talker Vivi, 47-year-old super-competitive Neil and 25-year-old media sales worker Charlotte. With risky menu choices and a fellow diner's peculiar laughter taking the spotlight, will the conversations ranging from lookalikes to riding polar bears be enough to secure Shanice the prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Retired estate agent Vivi hosts the second dinner party in and around Guildford. She is convinced her experience and original Scandinavian fayre can beat her less experienced competition and the soiree features Norwegian fish soup, ghost stories, ghost impressions, Norwegian singing and nursery rhymes

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sales manager Charlotte hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in and around Guildford, hoping to impress her guests enough to win the £1,000 prize money. However, she has taken a huge risk with an Asian-inspired menu, which could prove to be too exotic for entertainments manager David

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The penultimate day in and around Guildford, Surrey, sees children's entertainments manager David playing host. Having worked in the entertainments industry all his life, he is no stranger to showing people a good time, and employs his colleagues to put on a performance, while keeping his food simple. Is he forgetting the key ingredients of a dinner party?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final day in and around Guildford, Surrey, sales director Neil devises a sophisticated menu, dismissing his predecessors' efforts as unambitious. However, his unusual fayre is tempered by his choice of yodelling entertainment, which threatens to hinder his chance of scooping the £1,000 prize

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lee Juggurnauth jets off to the historic town of Mojácar in Spain's Almería region to help house-hunters Christine and Howard find the perfect holiday home. After 30 years of marriage and a few difficult years back at home, the couple have decided it's finally the right time to fulfil their long-held dream of buying a place in the sun. Lee sources five properties within their £50,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sports fan and rugby coach Matt loves all-things Italian and dreams of buying a holiday home near the historic city of Lucca in Tuscany. He's looking for a property with two bedrooms in a lively location, and brings along his best friend Shaun for some straight-talking advice. Jean Johansson secures the options of five properties for him to vivist within his £190,000 budget

programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help the Clayton-Fishers, who have fallen out of love with their home in Walthamstow, east London. Sarah and her wife Pepper have opposing views on what to do with their two-bedroom terrace, as Sarah thinks it is time to move on, but Pepper is digging her heels in and wants to convert the loft

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: In the seaside town of Weymouth, Dorset, the presenter helps a couple whose first-time family home was destroyed overnight by a flash flood. When they bought their 19th-century coach house in 2018, they had planned to renovate its higgledy-piggledy layout, but the overnight disaster means they've got a far bigger project than they bargained for. The architect also visits Chiswick, west London, to assist a couple whose master bedroom and bathroom he redesigned a couple of years ago. programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson heads to the glorious Greek island of Crete to help Jeanette and Phil, who are dreaming of starting a new, relaxed way of life in the sunshine while spending more quality time together. They're focusing their search around the beautiful, ancient city of Chania and ideally want a three-bedroom detached house with a pool. They have allocated a budget of £170,000, so Jean hunts out five properties for them to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Yvette and Agata call upon the services of Jasmine Harman in their hunt for their dream three-bed 'forever home' in the seaside town of Alvor on Portugal's Algarve. Property expert Jasmine rustles up five options for their budget of £300,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton goes house-hunting around the Messinia Peninsula in Greece with Martin and Sonia from Bristol. programme.desc: The week kicks off at The Earlsdale in Devon, where married couple Lewis and James hope their bold sense of style will prove a hit, although a dribbly shower rains on their chances

And it comes back to bite Sally and Jo at feedback time, as they accuse some of their rivals of tit-for-tat marking

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: In Dorset, newbie B&Ber Hilary aims to provide a home-from-home at her East Creek B&B - which doubles as her actual family home. The stunning coastal views instantly earn Hilary some brownie points, but a lavatory let-down leaves two guests with a dilemma. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to The Railway Ramblers' Rest in Devon, where hosts Nikki and Terry hope their unique take on self-catering will secure them the plaque. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Two families come to terms with life-changing events, including the relatives of eight-year-old Junior, who has an open leg fracture and suspected internal bleeding after being knocked off his bike by a car. His mum Bekki reveals how Junior learned to cope with extraordinary setbacks and how she worries about his future because of health problems of her own. Meanwhile, 46-year-old Anthony arrives in A&E after suffering chest pains during a day out at the races. As doctors attempt to get to the bottom of what's causing his dangerously high heart rate, his wife discusses how Anthony's awareness of his mortality was increased by losing his father

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Greg, 47, is airlifted to St George's with critical injuries to his arm and leg after being knocked off his motorbike on the way into work. As his wife Lucie rushes to his bedside, she's shocked by what she finds. Cameras also follow, Cara, 11, who has fallen from the top of a human pyramid doing gymnastics and has a broken arm that doctors must try to realign

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: This week's campers - Simon and Emmy, Sean and Marie, and Jen, Ian and Ellis - arrive at Blair Castle Caravan Park at the edge of the Cairngorms National Park

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Shannon Slater-Dent and Trevor Tredgett's Bove Town House in Glastonbury. The duo are keen to show off what their B&B is all about, so lead an afternoon of group sound healing. While Ben Davenport and Sian Dewaal from the Fritton Arms immediately recognise that this B&B will be tough competition, Stevie King and his head chef Dave from SugarBeat Eating House dismiss the activity as being 'hippy'. However, it's the poor night's sleep that bothers Kathryn and her best friend Trevor, from Treberthan B&B, as well as the fact they have to cook their own breakfast. At feedback time, the idea of self B&B comes under fire and Kathryn doesn't hold back with her sleep score

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Treberthan B&B in Par, Cornwall, where owner Kathryn Langley and her best friend Trevor Burnell set out to give their guests a taste of Cornish hospitality. Everyone's impressed by the high standard of cleanliness, but Stevie and David are put off by the old-fashioned decor. A Cornish pasty making competition reveals Kathryn's assertive streak, while professional chef David feels under pressure to win, Ben and Sian make an unconventional pasty, and Shannon and Trevor forget a vital ingredient. At breakfast, a request for goat's milk puts Kathryn under pressure

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Stevie King and his head chef David Lovett host at SugarBeat Eating House in Swainsthorpe in Norfolk. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Fritton Arms in Norfolk, run by Ben Davenport and partner Sian Dewaal. programme.title: Four in a Bed

However, the guests are less than impressed with the food, especially the main course - a curry made from frozen fish - but will a bit of belly dancing, Bollywood-style, help save the day? Meanwhile, singer Petrina and drummer Rob continue their flirting both at and away from the table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Drum tutor Rob hosts the third night in north Surrey, hoping his Thai-themed menu will impress his guests, as well as the object of his affections, Petrina. He serves prawns with lemon, lime and chilli for his starter, Thai chicken curry for his main, and rounds off with his own take on an ice-cream sundae. As a single man living in his stepmother's house, Rob thinks his competitors will see him as an underdog, so is intent on pulling out all the stops

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Singing teacher Petrina hosts the fourth dinner party in north Surrey, and she believes her experience of entertaining on cruise ships will give her the edge as she plans a showstopper of an evening. Will tonight be the night, or will he shy away when he discovers Petrina has sung in the West End and worked with dozens of household names?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

Becky is less than impressed with Giovanni's dishes, and matters go from bad to worse when he attempts to serve his home-made pasta. programme.desc: Courtney has been in love with her boyfriend for three years, but wonders if he's just a fantasy. Nev and Kamie cross digital borders to hunt the identity of her mystery man

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: Apryl fell for artist Daryl after years of him courting her by drawing portraits. Suspecting the art of deception, Nev and Kamie fear this case is going from sketches to sketchy

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: Scooba fell hard for Renae on a virtual social app, but soon after marrying her avatar she vanished! Now 6 years later, Renae has returned, but can Nev and Kamie track her down

programme.title: Skin A&E

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers
programme.desc: The pop-up parlour gets a visit from drag queen Devon, who feels snared by his barbed-wire tattoo as it is a painful reminder of an ex. Alice welcomes Halloween-loving Chippy, who has a spooky portrait of her daughter, Jay tackles an all-you-can-eat buffet tattoo, and Sketch helps Aidan, whose dislike of five pence shopping bags caused him to get carried away with his ink

programme.title: Geordie Shore

programme.title: Ridiculousness

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers

programme.title: The Great Fire
programme.desc: Dan Jones, Suzannah Lipscomb and Rob Bell take a fresh look at the Great Fire of London in 1666 by walking the actual route the fire took across the city, hour-by-hour and street-by-street, hoping to uncover what really happened during Britain's worst inferno. In this first edition, the three presenters investigate how the fire started, heading to Pudding Lane, site of the bakery where the blaze broke out. Dan and historian Dorian Gerhold then place a temporary 'blue plaque' on the very spot where the fire began

programme.title: The Great Fire
programme.desc: The team continues to walk the path of the infamous inferno, uncovering what really happened on the worst day of the four-day rampage. Dan Jones charts the destruction of the city's buildings, including London's spiritual home St Paul's Cathedral, which burned so fiercely its stones exploded. Suzannah Lipscomb reveals how Pudding Lane baker Thomas Farriner made sure no blame landed on him and Rob Bell finds out how the fire moved so fast

programme.title: The Great Fire
programme.desc: Documentary following the last day of the fire's rampage through the city of London in 1666, and the after-effects that changed the capital city for ever. The team investigate the treatment for burns victims, and how authorities dealt with the 100,000 refugees made homeless. Finally, they walk through some of the streets where the fire's impact still endures, and look at how London was rebuilt from the ashes

programme.title: Symbols of Evil
programme.desc: Exploring five examples of harmless symbols that have been turned into dangerous propaganda tools for movements that have hunted, tortured and killed, including the Hitler salute, the swastika, the Z of the Russian war against Ukraine, the Q of QAnon and the Star of David

programme.title: Heroes of the Somme
programme.desc: Military historian Dr Gavin Hughes traces the stories of seven of the men whose remarkable bravery won them the Victoria Cross, Britain's most prized military medal. Hughes travels to France to piece together their experiences, covering four key days of the battle and telling the story of the entire offensive, using a copy of a 100-year-old battle map to navigate the Somme landscape today and pinpoint the locations of individuals and the battalions they fought in

programme.title: WWI: The Last Tommies
programme.desc: Veterans of the First World War recall their experiences, with archive footage used to illustrate events, beginning with the first two years of the war in 1914 and '15. The men - aged 90 and more when interviewed - reveal how they survived and kept their spirits up in the trenches with death all around them, while there is a look at how families at home faced up to the absence and loss of husbands, fathers, sweethearts and son

programme.title: WWI: The Last Tommies
programme.desc: An immersive account of one of the most tragic battles in history, the Battle of the Somme, told through its last survivors and through families and communities at home who lost loved ones

programme.title: WWI: The Last Tommies
programme.desc: In the concluding episode, veterans look back on the First World War battles of 1917 and 1918, and reflect on how Britain and her Allies turned a looming defeat into victory. The veterans recall dramatic stories of Passchendaele in 1917, the German army's Spring Offensive in 1918, and the British troop's final push to victory

programme.title: America's Great War 1917-1918
programme.desc: The presence of American soldiers - nicknamed the 'Sammies' - on French soil during the Great War is virtually unknown. Yet two million arrived in France between 1917 and 1918 to fight on the bloody battlefields alongside French and British troops

programme.title: Pandemic - The Flu That Killed 50 Million
programme.desc: Docu-drama about the 1918 Flu pandemic, which killed more than 50 million people. Using dramatic reconstruction and eyewitness testimony from doctors, soldiers, civilians and politicians, this one-off brings to life the onslaught of the disease and the horrors of those who lived through it. Narrated by Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: Forts of Verdun
programme.desc: A journey inside the walls of the coveted 38 forts that were at stake during the bloodiest battle of the First World War using archival footage, 3D animations and interviews with historians and scientists

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. The body of an elderly woman is found in a cave, with her fingerprints burnt off and a posy of foxgloves at her side that she did not pick herself. programme.desc: The stories making the headlines both at home and abroad during the past seven days

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Mackenzie blows up at David, Levi fights to save a life, and Harper gets an unexpected message

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Cash reproaches Mackenzie for her outburst at David, and Tane's oxygen levels are plummeting, Levi and Jo are stumped on the cause of his worsening condition

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Dana shares the news with John over the arrest of her mother, and Justin reminds Lacey that her grief is still fresh and to give it time

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: A team of family and friends, Amy, JP, Nic and Alan, take on the Bridge of Lies, they must cross the Bridge, stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies. Hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Amanda & Alan's Italian Job
programme.desc: Amanda and Alan face their biggest challenge yet as they tackle the garden. The patch of land on the side of the mountain has been untouched for over a decade, and with no idea on what plants thrive in the Tuscan terrain, the pals seek help from gardening oracle Warrie Price, who has spent over 30 years cultivating a 15-acre oasis. Keen to inject authentic Tuscan design, Alan visits the ancient town of Impruneta in search of terracotta pots. Amanda calls in her green-fingered mum, but gets more than she bargained for when her parent takes a liking to a member of staff at a nearby garden centre

programme.title: Amanda & Alan's Italian Job
programme.desc: Alan Carr takes charge tackling the first bedroom, and inspired by the romance and history of his favourite city Florence, he is determined to have a mural. However, co-renovator Amanda Holden fears his creative juices could turn into a flood. To nail his aesthetic, Alan travels to the outskirts of the Tuscan capital to meet a woman whose medieval home is decorated in rare 14th-century frescoes, and is encouraged to take down the ceiling to reveal any original features. After realising the existing furniture is riddled with woodworm, the duo head to the Gulf of Poets for inspiration

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets adventurous couple Colin and Adele. They have employed a Swedish architect, an Italian interior design company and a Latvian building company to complete the eye-popping curved glass family home in the Manchester suburb where they both grew up

programme.title: Champions League Live
programme.desc: Paris Saint-Germain v Arsenal (Kick-off 5.00pm). Darragh Maloney presents coverage of the final from Puskas Arena in Budapest. The Gunners made it to their first final in this competition since 2006 thanks to a 2-1 semi-final victory over Atlético Madrid, with Bukayo Saka scoring the winner in the second leg at the Emirates. PSG beat Bayern Munich 6-5 across two epic encounters to reach their second successive Champions League final. The French club are looking to become the first back-to-back winners of the competition since Real Madrid won three titles in a row between 2016 and 2018. With analysis from Kevin Doyle and Richie Sadlier

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look into the lives of mammals, exploring how they have adapted to their changing surroundings and have found ways to create homes in the wild. The first episode focuses on animals that thrive in the dark, from leopards that use night vision to hunt their prey to bats using echolocation to navigate the night skies

programme.title: D-Day: 100 Days for Freedom
programme.desc: An examination of the 100 days it took for the Allies to liberate Normandy, to break through the German line and to pave the way towards victory. It was a period that made history, both heroic and tragic. The first of two programmes retraces events, day by day, from D-Day up to the Liberation of Paris, through a series of testimonies and colourised archives

programme.title: The Saturday Game
programme.desc: Action from the All-Ireland Senior Championship first-round matches, featuring Westmeath v Cavan at TEG Cusack Park and Armagh v Derry at BOX-IT Athletic Grounds

programme.title: Trump's Power & the Rule of Law
programme.desc: The high stakes showdown between President Donald Trump and the US courts over presidential power. Featuring insight from Trump allies, opponents and experts on how he tests the extent of his power, the legal pushback and impact on the rule of law

programme.title: JourneyCam International
programme.desc: Eleven-year-old Arian travels to Spain, discovering flamenco, cooking a family paella, and exploring her heritage

programme.title: news2day Weekly
programme.desc: The stories making the headlines both at home and abroad during the past seven days

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Remi apologises to Eden, Dana is over the moon at the positive news of Sonny's recovery, and Abigail is still unaware that Mali is using her garage as his workshop

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: David introduces Cash to the station's newest recruit - probationary constable Richard Brezniac. Roo presses Alf about booking his medical assessment

programme.title: Meet Me in New York (2022)
programme.desc: An ambitious events planner meets her match when a website editor hires her to organise his niece's birthday. Romantic drama, starring Brooke Nevin and Corey Sevier

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Preview
programme.desc: A look ahead to the tournament, which takes place in the USA, Mexico and Canada

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Preview
programme.desc: A look ahead to the tournament, which takes place in the USA, Mexico and Canada

programme.title: The Sunday Game Live
programme.desc: Monaghan v Mayo (Throw-in 4.00pm). Joanne Cantwell presents coverage of the All-Ireland Senior Football Championship first-round match at St Tiernach's Park. Monaghan qualified after finishing as runners-up to Armagh in the Ulster Senior Football Championship, and Mayo were semi-finalists in the Connacht Senior Football Championship. With analysis from Conor McManus and Lee Keegan

programme.title: The Spy Who Loved Me (1977)
programme.desc: James Bond teams up with a KGB agent to recover missing British and Soviet nuclear submarines, before whoever stole them can use their warheads. Unfortunately, 007 was responsible for the death of his new ally's lover - and she is determined to make him pay before their mission is complete. Spy adventure, starring Roger Moore, Barbara Bach, Curt Jurgens and Richard Kiel

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: Documentary showcasing the wonders, secrets and fragilities of the American continents, beginning with the creatures that live along North America's Atlantic coast, from the wild horses of Chesapeake Bay and the young raccoon learning the challenges of urban survival in New York City to the young black bears foraging in the Appalachian forests. Up in the air, bald eagles chase ospreys for a hard-won meal, while beneath the waters sand tiger sharks act as unlikely guardians for schools of scad fish. Narrated by Tom Hanks

programme.title: The Sunday Game
programme.desc: Des Cahill introduces highlights and discussion of first-round matches in the All-Ireland Senior Football Championship, including Dublin v Louth at Croke Park and Monaghan v Mayo at St Tiernach's Park

programme.title: Cloch Le Carn - Micheál
programme.desc: A portrait of GAA legend Micheal O Muircheartaigh, the voice of a nation, the king of commentators, who travelled the world promoting the game he loved. Featuring contributions from members of the O Muircheartaigh family, GAA legends Eddie Keher, Mick O'Connell, Cora Staunton, Kieran Donaghy and Eoin Liston, former president Mary McAleese and many more

programme.title: Shortscreen
programme.desc: A woman's very public break-up is saved when an Elvis impersonator comes to her rescue, and as the pair reminisce they discover meaning in their newly shared bond

programme.title: Blood Money: Inside the Soviet Economy
programme.desc: Two-part documentary exploring how the USSR - once seen as a second-rate industrial power, economically inferior to Germany, the USA and the UK - managed to defeat Hitler's armies and secure its place among the victors

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets adventurous couple Colin and Adele. They have employed a Swedish architect, an Italian interior design company and a Latvian building company to complete the eye-popping curved glass family home in the Manchester suburb where they both grew up

programme.title: Champions League Live
programme.desc: Paris Saint-Germain v Arsenal (Kick-off 5.00pm). Darragh Maloney presents coverage of the final from Puskas Arena in Budapest. The Gunners made it to their first final in this competition since 2006 thanks to a 2-1 semi-final victory over Atlético Madrid, with Bukayo Saka scoring the winner in the second leg at the Emirates. PSG beat Bayern Munich 6-5 across two epic encounters to reach their second successive Champions League final. The French club are looking to become the first back-to-back winners of the competition since Real Madrid won three titles in a row between 2016 and 2018. With analysis from Kevin Doyle and Richie Sadlier

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look into the lives of mammals, exploring how they have adapted to their changing surroundings and have found ways to create homes in the wild. The first episode focuses on animals that thrive in the dark, from leopards that use night vision to hunt their prey to bats using echolocation to navigate the night skies

programme.title: D-Day: 100 Days for Freedom
programme.desc: An examination of the 100 days it took for the Allies to liberate Normandy, to break through the German line and to pave the way towards victory. It was a period that made history, both heroic and tragic. The first of two programmes retraces events, day by day, from D-Day up to the Liberation of Paris, through a series of testimonies and colourised archives

programme.desc: Lord and Lady Fitzalan Howard, the owners of Carlton Towers near Selby in North Yorkshire, prepare to host a fitness boot camp weekend at their property - while also welcoming an array of guests to celebrate their son's birthday. Meanwhile, Sir Benjamin Slade hopes a murder mystery weekend at Maunsel House in Somerset will help pay off a sizeable electricity bill

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Life in the Devon fishing port of Brixham. Spring is in the air, and the town is a hive of activity as skippers return their pleasure yachts to the water after winter in the dry dock, while the pressure is on for Brixham to look its best for the arrival of Princess Anne, who will officially open the new £20million fish market, and the start of the summer season

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Brixham hosts its annual Pirate Festival, paying tribute to Devon's colourful maritime history, while fisherman Nathan sets off in search of a lucrative catch

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: Axe negotiates with a reluctant seller and Wendy makes a professional decision that adds further conflict to her relationship with Chuck

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: Axe assembles a war room in the Hamptons following a disastrous setback, and seeks counsel on how to recoup his losses. Drama, starring Damian Lewis

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: Axe investigates who was behind the breakdown in his Sandicot dealings, but faces formidable opposition. Chuck digs up dirt on a political rival, while Lara makes a PR trip to Sandicot to ease tensions between the town and the Axelrods. Wendy helps Taylor solve a problem at Axe Capital, and Chuck and Senior curry favour with a powerful ally

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Bullock and Swearengen contemplate a pre-emptive strike against Hearst, whose anger has been inflamed of late. However, the ruthless mining baron is preoccupied with Alma, whose bad habits lose her the bank services of Trixie, and possibly the affections of husband Ellsworth. Meanwhile, Aunt Lou Marchbank's long lost son arrives in town, but is soon sent packing. Starring Ian McShane

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Blazanov commits a violation for the good of the camp, and the elders hold a meeting, intending to form a plan to deal with ruthless gold baron Hearst. Meanwhile, the renovations at Langrishe's theatre are delayed. Drama, with Ian McShane, Brian Cox and Pavel Lychnikoff

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Bullock's letter is published in The Pioneer as the camp put their plan to deal with the villainous Hearst into action. Meanwhile, Wyatt Earp and his brother arrive in town as heroes after saving a sabotaged stagecoach - but there could be more to the newcomers than meets the eye. Drama, guest starring Gale Harold, with Timothy Olyphant

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Merrick has to explain why he published the Sheriff's letter, and Hearst's hired muscle terrorises the camp, forcing Bullock to issue contingency plans to Martha. Meanwhile, Wu is caught in a Pinkerton stampede and delivers a cryptic message to Al Swearengen. Starring Ian McShane and Timothy Olyphant

programme.title: Prisoner
programme.desc: Prison transport officer Amber's first day back after maternity sees her escorting ruthless contract killer Tibor Stone to court so he can testify in a high-profile case. When their convoy is attacked by a highly organised unit, Amber handcuffs herself to the prisoner to prevent his escape, but Tibor is a marked man and their assailants will stop at nothing to make sure he does not make it to court. Action thriller, starring Tahar Rahim and Izuka Hoyle

programme.title: Prisoner
programme.desc: Held captive at a secret weapons factory, Amber and Tibor narrowly escape execution by Nina before agreeing to a deal to help each other. Tibor leads Amber's husband Olly to a safe house, saving her family from Pegasus

programme.title: Prisoner
programme.desc: While being tracked down, Amber and Tibor turn the tables on Nina and hitch a ride to London in the back of a lorry, unaware that their driver has sold them out. Action thriller, starring Tahar Rahim and Izuka Hoyle

programme.title: Prisoner
programme.desc: Amber reunites with her family while Tibor faces his estranged mother, while Olly rejects their plan and contacts the National Crime Unit, leading Pegasus straight to them

programme.desc: The body of a girl is found with a broken rib which punctured her lung and 26 belt buckle marks on her body. Bayliss reveals his own childhood abuse to Pembleton.

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: The team investigate the killing of an Asian shopkeeper who tried to forbid dope dealers from operating outside his store. His killing leads detectives to Mahoney, whose parting shot to Kellerman throws the detective into a tailspin of depression

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Pembleton and Lewis are assigned to the murder of a wealthy woman. Each focuses on a different suspect - the victim's brother and an angry, talented art student from the poor side of town. A diamond ring with great sentimental value disappeared from the woman's finger after the body was found, and proves pivotal to the case

programme.title: The Guest Wing
programme.desc: Everyday life at Northumberland's Chillingham Castle, where the members of staff try to tactfully deal with the whims of their employer, baronet Humphry Wakefield, as the estate prepares to welcome a band of ghost-hunting tourists. Meanwhile, Carlton Towers in North Yorkshire hosts an annual dog show for Afghan hounds

programme.title: The Guest Wing
programme.desc: Lord and Lady Fitzalan Howard try to impress American guests at Carlton Towers with a programme of traditional activities, including skinning rabbits and taking high tea. Meanwhile, Sir Humphry Wakefield entertains tourists on a wine-tasting weekend at Chillingham Castle

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Teenager Logan starts work as an apprentice deck-hand, while landlady Pat recalls fond memories from her life in Brixham

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Fish merchant Robert and his wife Kelly decide to open a seafood restaurant in Brixham - but their determinedly contemporary approach to cuisine leaves some locals unimpressed. The programme also follows the journey of a turbot over 36 hours, as it is caught and auctioned off before being served in an exclusive London restaurant

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: As Hearst reveals his intentions for the upcoming elections, someone tries to murder Alma, so Swearengen takes the initiative and takes her into protective custody. Meanwhile, tension mounts among Langrishe's troupe over his interest in a new actor. Starring Ian McShane, Brian Cox and Molly Parker

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: In the penultimate episode in the series, another attempt is made on Alma's life, prompting her to seek refuge at the Gem. Meanwhile, Trixie decides to take matters into her own hands, leaving the rest of the camp to deal with what she's done, and Langrishe falls out with Hearst. Drama set in a lawless US town, starring Molly Parker, Brian Cox and Paula Malcomson

programme.title: Catherine the Great
programme.desc: After overthrowing her husband and having his successor jailed and eventually murdered, Russian empress Catherine II will stop at nothing to keep her position. She sees her own son as a threat to her and refuses to marry the man who helped her to power for fear he will share the crown. Historical drama following the German-born empress of Russia towards the end of her reign. Starring Helen Mirren and Jason Clarke

programme.title: Catherine the Great
programme.desc: Potemkin returns and hopes to begin a relationship with Catherine, only to discover she has fallen for Lieutenant Vasilchikov. Meanwhile, Prince Paul becomes increasingly frustrated at the perceived refusal of his succession

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Pembleton and Munch face trouble when they investigate an armed fugitive. Richard Belzer stars

programme.title: The Hangover Part II (2011)
programme.desc: Phil, Stu and Alan reunite in Bangkok for another stag weekend, but having learned the lessons of their previous misadventures, resolve to keep this one quiet and alcohol-free. Things inevitably do not go according to plan, and they must once again piece together the events of a wild night they cannot remember. Comedy sequel, starring Bradley Cooper, Ed Helms and Zach Galifianakis

programme.title: Transformers: The Last Knight (2017)
programme.desc: The shape-changing robots and their human allies face a confrontation with their original creator, who has brainwashed their leader and intends to save her own home planet by destroying Earth. To save the human race they must recover an ancient artefact from Arthurian legend - but their mission is threatened by old enemies as they uncover a terrible secret about the history of the world. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Mark Wahlberg, Anthony Hopkins, Gemma Chan, Stanley Tucci and John Turturro

programme.title: Final Score (2018)
programme.desc: An ex-soldier is forced into action when armed terrorists take control of a packed football stadium. Action drama, starring Dave Bautista, Pierce Brosnan and Ray Stevenson

programme.title: Clash of the Titans (2010)
programme.desc: The son of the Greek god Zeus is raised by humans and grows up to reject his immortal heritage in anger at the gods' cruel treatment of humans. He sets out on a quest to find a means to kill the evil lord of the underworld in revenge for his father's death, facing all manner of strange creatures. Mythological adventure, with Sam Worthington, Liam Neeson, Ralph Fiennes and Gemma Arterton

programme.title: The Amazing Spider-Man (2012)
programme.desc: A gifted teenager investigates the secrets hidden in his dead parents' past, leading him to a mysterious corporation and an incident that imbues him with superhuman powers. A personal tragedy drives him to use his new-found abilities to fight crime, and he is soon drawn into a deadly battle against a former colleague of his father, who has undergone a devastating transformation of his own. Superhero adventure reboot, starring Andrew Garfield, Emma Stone and Rhys Ifans

programme.title: The Amazing Spider-Man 2 (2014)
programme.desc: The webslinging hero battles a powerful new enemy when a downtrodden engineer develops lethal electrical powers, which he unleashes on those he believes have betrayed him. As if that wasn't enough to keep him busy, he faces an even greater threat when he is reunited with an unstable old friend who has been charged with a sinister mission by his dying father, leading to a series of tense confrontations that threaten to change his life for ever. Superhero adventure sequel, starring Andrew Garfield, Emma Stone, Jamie Foxx and Dane DeHaan

programme.title: The Karate Kid (2010)
programme.desc: An American youngster goes to live in China with his mother, where he becomes an outsider and falls foul of the local bullies. He receives help from the kindly school caretaker, who turns out to be a kung fu master and offers to train the boy to defend himself. Martial arts drama, with Jaden Smith and Jackie Chan

programme.title: Little Fockers (2010)
programme.desc: Nurse Greg is now the proud father of twins, but has yet to earn the respect of his wife's stern father after 10 years of marriage. The couple invite both sides of their extended family to the kids' birthday party, where Greg makes a desperate effort to finally prove his worth to his father-in-law. Comedy sequel, with Ben Stiller, Robert De Niro, Owen Wilson and Dustin Hoffman

programme.title: Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle (2017)
programme.desc: Four teenagers play a jungle-themed game on an old console, only to find themselves transported into the game and transformed into its heroic characters. They must embark on a death-defying journey through the game's wilderness setting to make it back to the real world, or be stuck as video game characters forever. Fantasy action comedy, starring Dwayne Johnson, Karen Gillan, Kevin Hart and Jack Black

programme.title: Jumanji: The Next Level (2019)
programme.desc: Players of Jumanji return, but as they attempt to rescue one of their own, they will have to brave parts unknown from arid deserts to snowy mountains, to escape the world's most dangerous game. Director Jake Kasdan's fantasy adventure sequel to his 2017 rebooted hit Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle, starring Dwayne Johnson, Karen Gillan, Kevin Hart and Jack Black

programme.title: Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again (2018)
programme.desc: It's five years since Sophie invited her three possible dads to her wedding, and now most of her family are reunited as she prepares for the grand reopening of the Hotel Bella Donna. Along the way, she learns more about her mother's past. Abba-themed musical sequel, starring Amanda Seyfried, Lily James, Pierce Brosnan, Cher and Andy Garcia

programme.title: The Hangover Part III (2013)
programme.desc: Drinking buddies Phil and Stu try to help their troubled friend Alan get his life in order, only to stumble into another bizarre misadventure. The Mob have taken Alan's brother hostage and to free him, they have to track down manic gangster Mr Chow, who has escaped from a Thai prison. Comedy sequel, starring Bradley Cooper, Ed Helms, Zach Galifianakis and John Goodman

programme.title: The Cinema List: Bingeable
programme.desc: On the Cinema List, join us as we explore our favourite franchises to get cosy with, from Transformers to Bad Boys 2. Snuggle down on the sofa and take your pick

programme.title: Just Go with It (2011)
programme.desc: A plastic surgeon pretends to be unhappily married to seduce women. When his girlfriend finds his prop wedding ring, he claims he is getting a divorce and has to persuade his assistant to pose as his soon-to-be ex-wife, but is forced to come up with yet more lies to maintain the illusion. Romantic comedy, starring Adam Sandler, Jennifer Aniston and Nicole Kidman

Struck by how much their lives have changed, they set out to relive the sexual misadventures of their adolescence, but only end up making bigger fools of themselves than they did as teenagers. Comedy sequel to the American Pie movies, starring Jason Biggs and Alyson Hannigan

programme.desc: Wisecracking toddler Mikey is joined by mischievous baby sister Julie and sibling rivalry inevitably erupts - while their parents have problems of their own to deal with, in the shape of the kids' good-for-nothing uncle. Comedy sequel, starring John Travolta and Kirstie Alley, with the voices of Bruce Willis and Rosanne Barr

programme.desc: When an experimental military weapon accidentally devastates Tasmania, volunteers travel to the island to help authorities identify the dead. Among them is a Ava Newman, who secretly hopes to find her missing husband. However, some of the bodies seem to be returning to life. Horror, starring Daisy Ridley, Brenton Thwaites and Mark Coles Smith

programme.desc: An eight-year-old girl experiences life on her family's Rhodesian farm during the final stages of the Bush War. Drama, written, directed by and starring Embeth Davidtz, with Lexi Ventner and Zikhona Bali

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview - May 2026
programme.desc: A look ahead at the films to enjoy this month on Sky Cinema

programme.title: The Shadow's Edge (2025)
programme.desc: A brilliant cyber-criminal gang evades the police's state-of-the-art surveillance system with a haul worth billions. Retired tracking expert Wong Tak-Chong is ushered back into active service to mentor rookie officer He Qiuguo and build an elite unit to stop the criminals. Action thriller, starring Jackie Chan, Zhang Zifeng and Tony Leung Ka-fai

programme.title: Honey Don't (2025)
programme.desc: California private detective Honey O'Donahue delves into a series of unusual deaths connected to a mysterious church. Co-writer/director Ethan Coen's comedy mystery, starring Margaret Qualley, Aubrey Plaza and Chris Evans

programme.title: Fuze (2026)
programme.desc: An unexploded Second World War bomb is discovered on a busy construction site in the centre of London. Chaos ensues as the military and police begin a mass evacuation. Drama, starring Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Theo James, Gugu Mbatha-Raw and Sam Worthington

programme.title: The Bride! (2026)
programme.desc: In 1930s Chicago, maverick scientist Dr Euphronius gives life to a murdered woman, sparking romance, police interest and radical change. Writer/director Maggie Gyllenhaal's horror based on The Bride of Frankenstein, starring Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale and Annette Bening

programme.title: Downton Abbey: The Grand Finale (2025)
programme.desc: In the summer of 1930, the Crawleys grapple with the threat of social disgrace when Mary finds herself at the centre of a public scandal. Romantic drama, starring Joely Richardson, Joanne Froggatt and Paul Giamatti

programme.title: Him
programme.desc: Cameron Cade is predicted to be the next great quarterback of American football. During a night-time training session, he suffers a traumatic brain injury. Supernatural horror, starring Marlon Wayans and Tyriq Withers

Action thriller, starring Jackie Chan, Zhang Zifeng and Tony Leung Ka-fai

programme.title: Dahmer on Dahmer: A Serial Killer Speaks

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A bizarre burglar who committed many crimes in many disguises and the fuel thief who fled the scene of the crime - with the petrol station owner clinging onto his car. Presented by Dom Littlewood

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A punchy pensioner's reaction is a surprise to himself, as well as his mugger. A man ploughs his car into a family home, but denies being the driver, and thieves feel like dancing

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: The famous Flying Squad chase old-school crooks known as 'the Manhole Men', and an inquisitive llama hastens the downfall of a farmyard felon

programme.title: Wirecard: A Billion Euro Lie
programme.desc: Documentary lifting the lid on the scandal that rocked the financial sector across the globe - the rise and billion-dollar fall of financial payment firm Wirecard

programme.title: When We Were Kings (1996)
programme.desc: Leon Gast's Oscar-winning documentary charting the challenge of an ageing Muhammad Ali for George Foreman's World Heavyweight title held in Zaire in 1974. The film explores the impact of Ali's visit to Africa before focusing on the epic fight, dubbed the 'Rumble in the Jungle', which has gone down in history as one of the greatest of all time. Spike Lee and Norman Mailer are among the contributors offering personal opinions on its importance to them

programme.title: Dogtown and Z-Boys
programme.desc: Documentary charting the history of skateboarding, from its 1970s emergence as a land-based form of surfing in Los Angeles and its subsequent explosion of popularity worldwide, to its current status as a widely-practised extreme sport. Narrated by Sean Penn, with contributions from experts Jay Adams and Tony Alva

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the Italian actress, one of the highest profile European stars of the 1950s and early '60s

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: The life and career of the English actor, who was nominated for Academy Awards for his roles in Mr Smith Goes to Washington, Casablanca, Mr. Skeffington and Notorious

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A profile of the American actress, who starred in Orson Welles' 1958 film Touch of Evil before achieving worldwide fame in 1960 for her role in Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho

programme.title: The Directors

programme.title: Sergio Leone: The Italian Who Invented America
programme.desc: Documentary paying homage to the legendary Italian director, featuring contributions from Clint Eastwood, Robert De Niro and Ennio Morricone

programme.title: Missile from the East (2021)
programme.desc: The true story of Ernst Degner, a champion East German motorcycle racer who risked his life defecting to West Germany during the Swedish Grand Prix in 1961

programme.title: I'm Chevy Chase and You're Not
programme.desc: Chevy Chase was a cast member on the inaugural series of Saturday Night Live and he built on that success to star in numerous Hollywood comedies and host the Academy Awards. He played hapless father Clark Griswold in the National Lampoon films and ran amok in pictures including Caddyshack, Spies Like Us, Fletch and Three Amigos. Two-time Emmy Award winner Marina Zenovich directs an authorised feature-length documentary portrait of Chase, which includes rare and intimate home movies and archive footage. Chase and his family share memories directly to camera, enriched with contributions from friends and collaborators including Dan Aykroyd, Beverly D'Angelo, Goldie Hawn, Lorne Michaels, Ryan Reynolds and Martin Short.

programme.title: I Am Richard Pryor
programme.desc: Documentary reflecting on the highs and lows of the comedian and actor's life, featuring interviews with Mike Epps, Tiffany Haddish and his widow Jennifer Lee

programme.title: My Icon
programme.desc: The England netball international talks about her idol

programme.title: Salt Path: A Very British Scandal
programme.desc: A look at Observer journalist Chloe Hadjimatheou's investigation into allegations of factual inaccuracies in Raynor Winn's book The Salt Path

programme.title: My Icon
programme.desc: British tennis star Heather Watson discusses the individuals who have inspired her over the course of her life

programme.title: Going Clear: Scientology and the Prison of Belief (2015)
programme.desc: Alex Gibney's documentary, based on Lawrence Wright's book, sheds light on the controversial religious movement, from its roots in the mind of founder L Ron Hubbard through to its rise in popularity in Hollywood and beyond. The film also profiles eight former members of the Church of Scientology, including Oscar-winning screenwriter and director Paul Haggis, who offer their perspective on the actions of the organisation's leaders past and present

programme.title: Chernobyl: The Lost Tapes

programme.title: The Directors

programme.title: The Man Who Stole The Scream
programme.desc: The extraordinary story of the theft of Edvard Munch's painting, The Scream, and a game of cat and mouse between an art-loving thief and a maverick Scotland Yard detective

programme.title: Man on Wire (2008)
programme.desc: Oscar-winning documentary about French street performer Philippe Petit. In 1974 he undertook a daring 45-minute tightrope walk and acrobatic routine on a wire stretched between the two towers of the World Trade Centre. Featuring re-creations of his earlier stunts at Paris's Notre Dame cathedral and Sydney Harbour Bridge

programme.title: Pantani: The Accidental Death of a Cyclist
programme.desc: Documentary about the rise and fall of Marco Pantani, the maverick Italian cyclist found dead less than six years after winning the Tour de France

programme.title: Elvis Presley: The Searcher
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary about the singer, beginning with his early life in Tupelo, Mississippi, and his unprecedented rise to fame over a single year

programme.title: Elvis Presley: The Searcher
programme.desc: Conclusion of the documentary about the singer, beginning with his return home after his discharge from the army, and how he dealt with a rapidly changing pop scene

programme.title: One to One: John & Yoko
programme.desc: Documentary examining the 18 months that John Lennon and Yoko Ono spent living in Greenwich Village in New York City in the early 1970s, featuring restored footage of Lennon's only full-length, post-Beatles concert

programme.title: Tina (2021)
programme.desc: Born Anna Mae Bullock in 1939, Tina Turner has sold in excess of 100 million records and earned numerous plaudits including Grammy awards and induction into the Rock And Roll Hall Of Fame with her first husband, Ike Turner. She was the first woman to grace the cover of Rolling Stone magazine and dabbled in acting including a memorable performance opposite Mel Gibson in Mad Max Beyond Thunderdome. Following a prolonged period of ill health, Turner intends to retire from the spotlight with her second husband, Erwin Bach. She bids farewell to her fans in the emotional documentary Tina. Directed by Dan Lindsay and TJ Martin, the film invites the singer to reminisce about her turbulent life and career with glowing contributions from friends, family and fans including Oprah Winfrey and Angela Bassett

programme.title: Jealous Guy: The Assassination of John Lennon
programme.desc: An exploration of the life and career of the Beatles superstar, with a look at the strange parallels between him and his killer Mark David Chapman

programme.title: The Stroll (2023)
programme.desc: The history of New York's Meatpacking District told from the point of view of transgender sex workers who lived and worked there

programme.title: Dettori (2021)
programme.desc: The story of arguably the world's greatest jockey, Frankie Dettori considered by many as the star of racing, and one of the most decorated sportsmen of all time

programme.title: Dave Not Coming Back (2020)
programme.desc: The story of two divers who embarked on a long and dangerous mission to retrieve a dead body from a freshwater cave in South Africa, with devastating consequences

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of American film-maker Jonathan Demme, who won an Academy Award for Best Director for The Silence of the Lambs

programme.desc: A team of scientists examines the remains of the Puma Punku temple in Bolivia in a bid to determine whether it could have been designed and constructed by extraterrestrials

programme.title: UFO Hunters
programme.desc: Events involving military and intelligence agencies, with suggestions of cover-ups relating to two unexplained incidents in the east of England in 1956 and 1980

programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: Two-part documentary exploring the Anglo-Norman invasion of Ireland. The first edition opens in the turmoil of 12th-century Ireland, where provincial kings compete for supremacy. When two of these power players drive a rival into exile, he seeks help from Henry II, the Anglo-Norman king of England. Henry's vassal Strongbow and other Norman allies then invade Ireland

programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: The second of the two-part documentary follows the course of the invasion, as Strongbow wins victory after victory. In 1171 Henry II arrived to takes control of Ireland, and he granted land there to settlers from Britain. Now archaeology and cutting-edge science reveal their fascinating lives. The episode also shows how the conquest of Ireland enabled centuries of war between England and France

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: Visits to the most remote, cold and inhospitable places on the planet to reveal great mysteries that have been frozen in time. This edition features a giant naval vessel located on the bottom of the Norwegian Sea and an ancient reptile-like creature found in the permafrost of Canada's Arctic. In Scotland, archaeologists are drawn to a strange, figure-eight shaped ditch, while an Antarctic research base finds itself suddenly surrounded by blood-soaked snow

programme.title: Canadian Pickers
programme.desc: Scott and Sheldon venture into cowboy country, meeting a collector of 1940s to '60s cowboy movie and TV memorabilia. Later, they discover a celebrity pilot's two hangars full of old planes, cars and bikes

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Theorists investigate a depiction of a stegosaurus found at Angkor Wat in Cambodia, comtemplating whether it suggest that alien species killed off the dinosaurs

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: A team of scientists examines the remains of the Puma Punku temple in Bolivia in a bid to determine whether it could have been designed and constructed by extraterrestrials

programme.title: Greatest Escapes of WWII
programme.desc: A look at some of the most incredible escapes of the conflict, as captured soldiers, sailors and airmen on both sides dared the impossible to win their freedom. They dug tunnels, stole planes, forced their way through deadly jungles, and marched huge distances pursued by death squads. This episodes focuses on Colditz Castle, the Great Escape and the bold Warburg Wire Job

programme.title: Bomber: Terror of WWII
programme.desc: In September 1939 German forces invaded Poland as four Luftwaffe bomber groups attacked Warsaw. By September 6, the city's anti-aircraft defences had been reduced to a few guns and much of the Polish capital was in ruins. The bomber aircraft dominated the skies throughout the rest of the Second World War as fleets stood ready on the airfields of every combatant nation

programme.title: Bomber: Terror of WWII
programme.desc: On September 1 1939 German forces invaded Poland as four Luftwaffe bomber groups attacked Warsaw. By September 6 the city's anti-aircraft defences had been reduced to a few guns and much of the Polish capital was in ruins. The bomber aircraft dominated the skies throughout the rest of the Second World War as fleets stood ready on the airfields of every combatant nation

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.sub-title: Invincible - The Royal Navy's Greatest Ship
programme.desc: Raksha Dave and Alex Langlands examine the history of HMS Invincible, which was captured from the French in 1747 and helped transform the Royal Navy with the lessons it taught about organisation and ship design. For more than 260 years, HMS Invincible lay at the bottom of the Solent, but archaeologists are now diving beneath the waves in a £2m excavation to discover more about this game-changing ship

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: Archaeologists conduct a dig at Auckland Castle in Co Durham, which 700 years ago was home to a group of power-crazed bishops who behaved more like warriors than religious leaders. The experts head to Dirleton Castle in East Lothian to reveal the role one of the men played in defending England from William Wallace's army, and also try their hands at the art of stained glass-making, and fire a working trebuchet - the ultimate medieval weapon of war

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: Centuries ago, canals were the liquid highways of Britain, transporting raw materials and finished goods all over the country. Their arrival kick-started the Industrial Revolution, which was to turn Britain into a superpower. The very first water superhighway was the Bridgewater Canal, completed in 1761 to carry coal to Manchester from the mines in Worsley. Here, a team of archaeologists from Salford University roots out this industrial past, working with volunteers to excavate the very coal mine that inspired the creation of the canal

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Theorists investigate a depiction of a stegosaurus found at Angkor Wat in Cambodia, comtemplating whether it suggest that alien species killed off the dinosaurs

programme.title: Ancient Aliens

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: The role of ships during the Cold War, featuring a look at two submarines - former enemies - now moored in a Kent river. Plus, the story of Soviet submariner Vasili Arkhipov, who refused to authorise a nuclear launch

programme.title: Combat Trains
programme.desc: Exploring how Lawrence of Arabia used guerilla warfare against the Ottoman Empire's railways. Plus, two secrets hidden under New York's Grand Central Terminal - President Roosevelt's personal train that brought him secretly into the city, and a M42 generator that it is claimed Nazi spies tried to destroy

programme.title: Combat Machines
programme.desc: Machines that would have been used had a stand-off resulted in all-out nuclear war, from the smallest tactical nuke to an experimental flying jeep

programme.title: Cursed Histories
programme.desc: More instances of the granting of so-called 'curses'. This episode highlights the Egyptian royals who promised turmoil for anyone who dares disturb their 'eternal rest'. Plus, a devilish figure at the Mississippi crossroads who granted great musical talents in exchange for a bluesman's soul and the pantheon of gods with the apparent power to punish any accursed name carved into dark tablets of ancient lead

programme.title: Cursed Histories
programme.desc: Elements, places, icons or events that seem to be plagued by what can only be described as a 'curse'. This episode focuses on a 'damned' amethyst gem, a devilish beast and the downfall of a family for several generations.

programme.title: Mysteries Unearthed with Danny Trejo
programme.desc: From priceless treasure found inside a lost city, to an ancient manuscript hidden behind a wall, Danny explores mysterious secrets lost deep inside various unseen chambers, including tunnels, caves and burial sites

programme.desc: The invasion of Poland by the German army in 1939, examining how Hitler succeeded in pushing through his rearmament and war plans relatively unhindered

programme.title: Bomber: Terror of WWII

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: Raksha Dave and Alex Langlands search for the remains of a First World War stable at Catterick Garrison in North Yorkshire, where thousands of horses would have been housed and trained for the front line. They join a team comprised of archaeologists and the military attempting to uncover the role this building played in the war effort. They also visit Britain's oldest cavalry regiment, examining tools that soldiers would have used to look after their steeds, and hear the story of Warrior, known as the horse the German's could not kill

programme.title: Ancient Superstructures
programme.desc: The Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem is regarded by many as the most sacred Christian site in the world. Every year, the site welcomes more than one million pilgrims. Built on the site of the death and resurrection of Jesus of Nazareth, it has a unique atmosphere. Defied by time, wars and earthquakes, the Holy Sepulchre was rebuilt many times, and has transformed itself into an architectural labyrinth. Here, thanks to the use of new technologies, the Holy Sepulchre reveals its secrets as never before

programme.title: Ancient Superstructures
programme.desc: A walk through India's Taj Mahal, the iconic mausoleum complex commissioned in 1632 by the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan to house the remains of his beloved wife

programme.title: Battle 360
programme.desc: The Japanese attack on the USS Enterprise, which came at a point in the Second World War when the vessel was the only battle-ready American carrier left in the Pacific

programme.desc: In a Bristol graveyard, Tori Herridg and the team investigate the grisly discovery of sliced-open human skulls and explore their connection with a local hospital, a celebrated surgeon, and the crime of body-snatching

programme.title: Alien Corridors
programme.desc: Built on swamp and sandy loam, all that grows in the Pine Barrens are the weeds of civilisation. However, there seems to be something about this barren land with its pygmy pines, high winds and frequent foresters that attracts alien attention

programme.desc: The case against King John, also known as John Lackland, who had a reputation for being a cruel and treacherous ruler. He was known for his ruthlessness, including the deaths of his nephew and the imprisonment and starvation of others

programme.title: Battle 360
programme.desc: The naval attack on the Solomon Islands, which was the first American invasion of the Second World War, is reconstructed using CGI technology

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise & Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: Hitler's invasion of the Soviet Union on June 22, 1941. His aim was to weaken Britain in Europe and gain 'lebensraum', or living space, in the East. The Nazi regime gradually advanced its so-called 'final solution to the Jewish problem' culminating in the appalling events associated with the Holocaust

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: The actor is joined by astrophysicist Sarah Cruddas to recall the events of October 2017, when astronomers at the Pan-STARRS observatory saw an object they named Oumuamua, the very first observed visitor from interstellar space. Craig and Sarah talk to Senior Astronomer Dr Seth Shostak of the SETI Institute, who shares his theories of what Oumuamua could possibly be and postulates that the very fact it entered the solar system at all is the best argument of all for it being alien

programme.title: Conspiracy Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: Investigating the plans and goals of the world's most secretive organisations, considering whether elite groups of power players could be plotting the fate of the world

programme.title: Conspiracy Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: Investigating the truth hidden inside the US government's secret and highly fortified areas, from Fort Knox to Hangar 18 and Camp Hero

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: River detectorist Beau Ouimette, TV presenter and keen swimmer Rick Edwards, and underwater archaeologist Gary Bankhead delve into the depths of Britain's watery past. Here, the trio find evidence from one of Scotland's bloodiest eras as they wade through the waters of the River Garry in the stunning Scottish Highlands

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Antarctica may serve as home base for extraterrestrial visitors to Earth, but as human exploration of the frozen continent increases, humans may come face to face with the ruins of an ancient advanced civilization buried beneath the ice. This episode explores the icy wilderness to find out whether the claims are true

programme.title: Knut: The Viking Emperor
programme.sub-title: Knut the Great, Master of Northern Europe
programme.desc: The documentary traces Knut's rise from young warrior to North Sea ruler through Assandun, the taking of Denmark and Norway and his grip on England

programme.title: Hells Angels: Kingdom Come
programme.sub-title: The Hells Angels, the Mafia and El Chapo
programme.desc: An inside look at how the Hells Angels claimed their throne - and the war that is raging across their territories. When war breaks out between the Hells Angels and an enemy, the Mafia stepped in. An unholy alliance formed with El Chapo, leading to a deadly end for a Hells Angels kingpin

programme.title: Hells Angels: Kingdom Come
programme.desc: How a raging war with the Bandidos forced the Hells Angels to form an unexpected alliance with a former rival. A new threat in 2021 leads to a once-unthinkable alliance

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the history of Warwick Castle, which started as a motte and bailey fort established by William the Conqueror in 1068 and was transformed into a stone fortress two centuries later by the Beauchamp family. He examines the role Guy Beauchamp played in the kidnapping and murder of one of Edward II's closest allies and descends into the castle's claustrophobic dungeon

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The Pickers look for hidden memorabilia by digging through junk piles, abandoned barns and neglected garages. Mike Wolfe and Jersey Jon turbo-pick a mint-condition stockpile in Tennessee. Later, in Kentucky, they unearth a bonanza of vintage treasures at a 1950s drive-in

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick is ready for some football when a Super Bowl bucket hat lands at the shop. Spencer brings in an Incredible Hulk comic book, while a set of Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs pencil holders awakens Corey's interest

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Researchers ask if cloud computing represents the rediscovery of an ancient repository of knowledge known as the Akashic Record. Neuroscientist Dr Dario Nardi measures the brainwaves of Dr Deepak Chopra as he enters a state of deep meditation

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos studies India's Sanskrit texts, which are filled with descriptions of brain surgery, flying crafts, nuclear physics, and other advanced sciences. He learns that Hindu scholars attribute this knowledge to a time when gods walked among humans

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Tom goes for one final ride on the trapline to pull his last sets before passing the torch to Sean and Hank. Jake has a showdown for the ages when three lions are cornered in a single tree, Marty makes a last ditch run at the wolverine that has decimated his trapline all season, while Lauro explores the Arctic sea ice by dog team and hunts caribou on the frozen tundra. Mike races to rebuild his cookhouse in the wake of a devastating bear invasion

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.sub-title: Tom Oar's Secrets from the Wild
programme.desc: Trapper Tom Oar shares his hard-earned survival secrets forged through a lifetime of experience living in the wild, as he concludes his association with the series

programme.title: The Curse of Oak Island
programme.desc: The team makes a find in the Money Pit that offers new evidence of a possible Templar tie to the Oak Island mystery

programme.title: Storage Wars

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Ivy kidnaps Pops and takes him to Rosamond, where they deftly score a fine locker. Kenny sets out to brighten everyone's day and his clowning around has him seeing stars. Lisa proves she has a friend - for now

programme.title: The Mystery of the Holy Lance
programme.desc: Mentioned in the Gospel of John, the Holy Lance is known as the weapon used by Roman legionary Longinus to pierce Christ's side after his crucifixion. According to legend, whoever possesses it becomes invincible and capable of ruling the world. Throughout history, the Lance have been sought after by many ambitious leaders, such as Emperor Constantine, Napoleon, Barbarossa and Hitler. This documentary combines scientific investigation with the latest historical research to explore the 2,000 year-long history of this captivating artefact

programme.title: Pharaohs at War
programme.sub-title: Thutmose III - The Battle of Megiddo
programme.desc: Documentary detailing decisive confrontations between pharaohs and their greatest rivals in Ancient Egypt, which defined the legacy of one of history's most iconic civilisations. This episode focuses on the Battle of Megiddo which, fought in 1457 BC, was a significant military engagement led by Pharaoh Thutmose III against a Canaanite coalition. It resulted in a decisive Egyptian victory, solidifying their dominance in the Near East

programme.title: Pharaohs at War
programme.sub-title: Ramses II - The Battle of Kadesh
programme.desc: The Battle of Kadesh, fought in 1275 BCE between the Egyptian Empire led by pharaoh Ramesses II and the Hittite Empire led by king Muwatalli II. In one of the world's largest chariot battles, fought beside the Orontes River, Ramses sought to wrest Canaan and Syria from the Hittites and recapture the city of Kadesh. There was a day of carnage as approximately 5,000 chariots charged into the fray, but no outright victor. Ultimately, the battle led to the world's first recorded peace treaty

programme.title: Pharaohs at War
programme.sub-title: Ptolemy IV - The Battle of Raphia
programme.desc: The Battle of Raphia, fought in 217 BC near modern Rafah, which was a pivotal clash between Ptolemaic Egypt and the Seleucid Empire during the Fourth Syrian War. The outcome was a decisive victory for Ptolemy IV Philopator's forces, which secured Coele-Syria for Ptolemaic Egypt

programme.title: Pharaohs at War
programme.desc: A look at the Battle of Actium in 31 BC, a decisive naval engagement that pitted the forces of Octavian against those of Roman politician and general Mark Antony and Egyptian ruler Cleopatra. This clash, near the Roman colony of Actium in Greece, marked the end of the Roman Republic's civil war era and the beginning of the Roman Empire under Octavian's rule

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts spends a year visiting archaeological digs around the country, finding out about discoveries that shed new light on British history. In a Roman-themed first edition, she examines the mystery surrounding the skeletons of 97 babies found beside the Thames, a newly discovered town in rural Devon and a cult figure buried for 1,700 years beneath a fort

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores archaeological digs at sites dating back to the Viking age, offering an insight into how the seafaring warriors from Scandinavia lived across Britain. In the Outer Hebrides, she examines signs of the earliest Norse settlers, before inspecting a collection of silver and gold pieces discovered in York. There is also a look at some of the most treasured Viking finds, including 12th-century ivory chess figures the Lewis Chessmen

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War
programme.desc: Despite the Germans effort to protect France, the Allies' invasion ended in triumphant liberation. Here, some of the brave soldiers involved reveal their first-hand stories

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War
programme.desc: The Allies continued to push from the west to Berlin, while survivors recount the horrors behind finding Germany's concentration camps

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War
programme.desc: The war in the Pacific nears its violent end as a drastic tactic triggers the nuclear age, and the brave men share their thoughts behind the tragic events

programme.title: Mysteries Unearthed with Danny Trejo

programme.title: Strange Evidence

programme.title: Flying Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the world of the pterosaur, a flying creature that occupied the skies 200 million years ago. Visiting sites around the world, from the Dorset coast to New Mexico, he explains how the reptiles evolved to defy the forces of gravity, and examines the features of their anatomy that can still be seen in present-day descendents. He also discovers why they became extinct after dominating the skies for millions of years

programme.title: David Attenborough's Dragons and Damsels
programme.desc: Arguably more beautiful than butterflies, better fliers than hummingbirds and with intriguing and complex behavioural patterns - dragonflies and damselflies are marvels of the insect kingdom. This documentary uses the latest camera technology to show their manoeuvres, mating strategies, territorial behaviour and flight dynamics, revealing them to be the ultimate insect
