programme.title: Sacred Rivers with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer travels to China for a trip along the Yangtze, Asia's longest river and the third longest in the world. He follows the route to Dazu, where he sees 50,000 ancient rock carvings, some of the only religious artefacts not to have been destroyed under communist rule. He then takes a river cruise to the Three Gorges Dam - the biggest power station on the planet - before ending his journey in Shanghai, a shining symbol of China's new economic might and a city where many are rediscovering religious faith

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the explorer travels through Cornwall as its emerges from the first lockdown, discovering the impact of Covid-19 on a part of the country highly reliant on tourism. Simon discovers the ongoing impact of the collapse of the tin-mining industry in the 1980s when he visits one of Britain's poorest estates, and meets the entrepreneurs who sell homemade food on the beach who are struggling to make enough money to last the winter

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the conclusion of his two-part journey, Simon investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country. He discovers how the region's farmers are adopting new, less intensive approaches that are both more profitable and environmentally friendly, and reveals how reintroducing beavers to the uplands could tackle the problem of widespread flooding. He also encounters some of the county's iconic wildlife, including grey seals that live in constant risk of getting caught up in discarded fishing nets

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie returns to where his boating life began, exploring London's canals on Naughty Lass. He travels through Little Venice and moors in Paddington Basin

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: In 1930, recovering from a personal crisis, Agatha Christie fell in love with and married Max Mallowan. This relationship, and the security it brought, saw Agatha enter the most prolific and successful chapter of her career. This would be the period in which Agatha penned some of her most famous and enduring bestsellers, including Murder on the Orient Express. Lucy follows in Agatha's footsteps to discover the roots of these works and explores how, in later life, Agatha achieved global celebrity - but remained very much the anonymous observer, hiding in plain sight

programme.title: Equator
programme.desc: Simon Reeve heads to Indonesia, the world's largest archipelagic nation, which is home to 220 million people speaking 700 different languages on a collection of 18,000 islands. He learns about the severe threat to orang-utans in Borneo, hears rumours about widespread killings of immigrants and is adopted by a local man. However, disaster strikes when the explorer and his crew head back to the mainland

programme.title: Equator
programme.desc: Concluding his journey, Simon Reeve visits South America, where he learns how the ecosystem of the Galapagos Islands is under threat from tourists, and meets remote indigenous tribes in the Amazon rainforest. He also travels through the most dangerous and lawless area of Colombia, before risking his life surfing a mini-tsunami in the waters surrounding Brazil

programme.title: The Tea Trail With Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve travels to East African countries Kenya and Uganda to meet some of the millions of people involved in producing tea and learn more about the processes involved

programme.title: Bangers & Cash

programme.title: Bangers & Cash

programme.title: Bangers & Cash

programme.title: Mega Metro
programme.desc: An extension is now underway to get people into the centre of Australia's biggest city Sydney, including a new tunnel-crossing deep under the harbour

programme.title: Mega Metro
programme.desc: We go back under Sydney Harbour to check out history in the making as the construction of the twin metro rail tunnels continues deep under the surface

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Guided by his Bradshaw's 1913 Handbook of Indian, Foreign and Colonial Travel, Michael Portillo embarks on a rail journey from Amritsar to Shimla. Along the way he helps to feed people at the world's largest free kitchen, and travels the railway routes used by millions of migrants during Partition. Beginning in the Sikh holy city, Michael is dazzled by the beauty of the Golden Temple and awed by the scale of its langar - the world's largest free kitchen. His route then takes him through the Punjab, and at Kalka, Michael glimpses the Himalayas for the first time

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Jodhpur to Delhi, heading into the desert to take a camel ride to a village where life has changed little in centuries, before embarking on the Jaipur-Agra-Delhi 'Golden Triangle' tour - India's must-do itinerary in 1913 as today. Michael also visits Agra, where he learns how the Taj Mahal, built by a Mughal emperor, was restored by a British viceroy at the time of his Bradshaw's guide book

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Bradshaw's 1913 Handbook of Indian, Foreign and Colonial Travel leads him on railway journey through the modern south Indian states of Karnataka and Tamil Nadu. He travels from the former princely state of Mysuru, aka Mysore, to the first stronghold of the East India Company in Chennai, formerly Madras

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: As Robbie Cumming leaves the scenic Llangollen Canal, he faces a journey of running repairs and narrowboat maintenance on the old Naughty Lass

programme.title: Sacred Rivers with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer travels to China for a trip along the Yangtze, Asia's longest river and the third longest in the world. He follows the route to Dazu, where he sees 50,000 ancient rock carvings, some of the only religious artefacts not to have been destroyed under communist rule. He then takes a river cruise to the Three Gorges Dam - the biggest power station on the planet - before ending his journey in Shanghai, a shining symbol of China's new economic might and a city where many are rediscovering religious faith

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the explorer travels through Cornwall as its emerges from the first lockdown, discovering the impact of Covid-19 on a part of the country highly reliant on tourism. Simon discovers the ongoing impact of the collapse of the tin-mining industry in the 1980s when he visits one of Britain's poorest estates, and meets the entrepreneurs who sell homemade food on the beach who are struggling to make enough money to last the winter

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the conclusion of his two-part journey, Simon investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country. He discovers how the region's farmers are adopting new, less intensive approaches that are both more profitable and environmentally friendly, and reveals how reintroducing beavers to the uplands could tackle the problem of widespread flooding. He also encounters some of the county's iconic wildlife, including grey seals that live in constant risk of getting caught up in discarded fishing nets

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie returns to where his boating life began, exploring London's canals on Naughty Lass. He travels through Little Venice and moors in Paddington Basin

Despite its proximity to the busy airport, the Waterhall Country House Hotel is struggling and owners Anne and Andy Marsh desperately need a change in fortunes if it is to become profitable. Can Alex set them on the way to success?

In the second part, he discusses becoming only the second player to reach 200 appearances for Munster and how he dealt with anxiety and mental health throughout his rugby career

Meanwhile, Dr Makori fears for her relationship with Dr Fox when he tells her he has been offered a job in Biafra. Medical drama, with Andy Wear, Paul Fox and Kananu Kirimi

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A wedding reception held on Elsinby Pier ends in tragedy when a fishing trawler sails badly off course. Meanwhile, Adam faces up to a tough decision and the staff at the hospital prepare for a new arrival as Jill goes into labour. Robert Cavanah and Amy Robbins star

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson helps sisters Sharon and Joy with their struggling country house hotel in Worlington, Suffolk. While the Grade-II listed building is in good repair, the operating systems are chaotic and the menu too extensive for the solitary chef to handle. Ruth organises a revamp of the dated-looking function room - prompting the delighted owners to plan a wedding fair in their new-look space

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade visits southern Africa to see if he can track down a deadly fish that lurks in the eerie waters of the Okavango. A killer with shark-like teeth, there are stories of it hunting in packs and feeding on schooling fish or even human bodies. Jeremy is determined to find out if it is a giant piranha or something equally terrifying

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: A lurcher arrives at Battersea Dogs and Cats Home with a terrible injury in need of urgent surgery. Plus, Motherland stars Anna Maxwell Martin and Diane Morgan help Paul with a cavalier King Charles spaniel, and calm down an over excited Frenchie pup

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines real-life cases, looking at the events leading up to crimes, going through the police investigations and summing up the trials

programme.desc: Documentary following life in the Spanish resort, this time at the Hotel Benidorm Plaza as Easter sees the start of the new holiday season. Checking in this week is a female rugby team from Manchester looking to let off some steam, a couple returning to relive their honeymoon, and friends hoping for solace in the sun

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: As the team at the Hotel Benidorm Plaza prepare to throw a party to show off the hotel to local travel agents, the Spanish resort is hit by a rare rainstorm, resulting in the hotel springing leaks and causing the electricity to cut out. Meanwhile, a 14-strong friendship group party in memory of their dear friend, and a couple celebrate their 25th wedding anniversary at Benidorm's famously romantic waterfalls

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade takes on Africa's wild Zambezi River to find an unknown predator dragging fishermen to their deaths. Crocodiles and hippos are guilty of killing dozens of victims in plain view, yet it is said there is another monstrous beast stalking these deep and turbulent waters. Locals speak of 'the Mazunda' - but what is this sinister, child-swallowing creature?

programme.desc: Presenter Helen Skelton and expert garden designer Danny Clarke bring unloved gardens back to life in a single day using clever design, imaginative ideas and recycling or re-use of existing plants and materials. Each show sees youngsters filmed while taking part in a variety of classroom activities and games, unaware that we're actually testing everything from empathy and lateral thinking to curiosity and rule breaking

programme.desc: US reality series. The drama at Danielle's mozzarella party eases after an olive branch from Teresa, but Louie and Joe soon cause it to return.

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi comes to the rescue of more struggling hotels, beginning with a visit to Epstein House in Liverpool where owner Patrick Duggan is having problems keeping his business afloat. The property was the home of the Beatles' manager Brian Epstein and Patrick was sure the guest house would be highly profitable when he bought it, but such expectations have yet to be realised

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Cassie and Sam prepare to celebrate their first wedding anniversary. Cassie welcomes Grey House guest Joy, who seems to be spying on the Merriwicks. Fantasy, starring Catherine Bell

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi tries to reverse the fortunes of Warwick Hall in Cumbria, one of her grandest hotels yet, but one suffering from low occupancy and negative feedback. While Alex is initially impressed with what she finds, she soon begins to discover the cracks and realises that she has a few harsh home truths to deliver - can she convince the owners that they need to rebrand themselves as a B&B instead of a hotel?

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Jenny 'The Vixen' Ryan, Anne 'The Governess' Hegerty, Shaun 'The Dark Destroyer' Wallace, Mark 'The Beast' Labbett and Darragh 'The Menace' Ennis come together to take on more contestants from across the country. Can any of them come out on top? Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi offers advice to the owner of the Westward Ho! in Folkestone, who was left this 13-bed hotel in his mother's will 20 years ago. He has fallen out with the young couple who live in and manage the property, while profits are dropping as occupancy is low and the hotel is in constant need of repair

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Towie star James 'Arg' Argent calls upon girlfriend Lydia Bright to help him throw a dinner party for four strangers at his Essex home. Brian McFadden hosts the game show where diners try to guess the identity of a mystery host holding a dinner party in order to win a cash prize

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The five-time Paralympian talks about her life as an elite swimmer and her journey to gold in Rio 2016

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Magazine programme blending lifestyle interviews and news content with human interest stories, consumer affairs, health, politics, gardening, education, finance, food and fashion

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner demonstrate dishes for a festive season table menu, including smoked salmon and seeded soda bread, chicken vol au vent, a Christmas stuffing cauliflower, and gravy carrots. Plus, a festive chocolate cake that is a twist on a Black Forest gateau

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: As the Covid-19 pandemic grips the nation, cameras follow life at Heathrow, as depleted staff members fight to keep the airport going. Customer service team member Demi is on hand to offer help to all those who are allowed to travel in and out of the airport, and has his work cut out as he tries to track down a missing passenger. Elsewhere, staff adjust to the new safety measures put in place to stop the spread of the virus

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. This time, the iconic Boeing 747 flies out of the airport for the final time, with plane spotters around the world hoping to bid it a fond farewell. Elsewhere, new cargo driver Dylan hopes his love for hypnosis can get him out of a jam

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard asks the questions as Harry Judd, Patrick Grant and Angela Barnes answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Cherry Healey, Dallas Campbell, Ria Hebden and Robert Peston pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts another charity edition of the game show, with Stephen Mangan, Kemi Rodgers and Tom Allen all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

programme.title: Week in Review
programme.desc: A look back at the main local, national and international stories of the week

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts as a contestant tries to open the 22 red boxes in the right order, taking on the infamous Banker for the chance to win a life-changing cash prize

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: After weeks of auditions and five semi-finals, the national talent search comes to a close as the remaining acts perform one last time. Their aim is to win a slot at this year's Royal Variety Performance and get their hands on the £250,000 cash prize. All they have to do is impress the voting public, as the opinions of judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI count for nothing - who wins is entirely down to the viewers at home. Presented by Ant and Dec

programme.title: Nobody's Fool
programme.desc: With fewer players left and the finale looming, alliances tighten and betrayals feel inevitable, while Harry reveals himself to be great at maths and this has not gone unnoticed. Whispered conversations, overheard plots and shifting loyalties dominate the house as players begin thinking not about survival, but about who they want to face in the final. A maths/shooting-led challenge rewards bold, decisive play from Billy and delivers a powerful advantage. Strategic reality quiz, hosted by Emily Atack and Danny Dyer

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by reality star and businesswoman Kim Kardashian and award-winning actress Sarah Paulson, who are appearing together in the new TV legal drama All's Fair. Breaking Bad star Bryan Cranston chats about taking to the West End stage in All My Sons, while actress and singer Rachel Zegler also appears, fresh from wowing audiences at the London Palladium in Evita. Plus, Little Simz performs Lion, from her album Lotus

programme.title: Matthew Perry: A Hollywood Tragedy
programme.desc: Friends star Perry's sudden death in October 2023 shocked the world. A known addict, he was found to have ketamine in his system and months later five individuals were charged with illegally supplying him the drug. This documentary explores Perry's rise to fame and his tragic final days with insights from castmates, friends, law enforcement and Perry's own candid accounts

programme.title: Protection
programme.desc: After a shooting at a safe house, witness protection officer Liz has many questions to answer, including why the officer she was having an affair with was also at the crime scene. Her secret investigation is threatened by pressure from higher-ups, but some disturbing truths lead her further into the conspiracy

programme.title: Code Blue: Murder
programme.desc: South Wales Police's Major Crimes Unit examine three cases, two killings and one assault, in which one punch had a devastating impact. programme.desc: Ben Shephard invites celebrities to take part in a special edition of the quiz show, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.title: Is Your Smartphone Spying on You?

programme.title: The Way
programme.desc: With the town under martial law, Dee and Geoff try to find Owen who, denied medication, is hallucinating in an isolation camp. Thea discovers that a new security unit at the police station is trawling footage of the protest, and she notices that Owen is being singled out. Husband Dan is increasingly concerned about getting his family out. With Luke Evans

programme.title: The Full Monty (1997)
programme.desc: Six unemployed men from Sheffield decide to become strippers for one night only in a bid to raise extra money. However, with no dancing experience and far from perfect physiques, the men rely on the unique selling point of complete nudity to impress the punters. Comedy, starring Robert Carlyle, Mark Addy, Tom Wilkinson, Hugo Speer and Paul Barber

programme.title: The Six O'Clock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: Protection

Her secret investigation is threatened by pressure from higher-ups, but some disturbing truths lead her further into the conspiracy

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi offers advice to the owner of the Westward Ho! in Folkestone, who was left this 13-bed hotel in his mother's will 20 years ago. He has fallen out with the young couple who live in and manage the property, while profits are dropping as occupancy is low and the hotel is in constant need of repair

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Towie star James 'Arg' Argent calls upon girlfriend Lydia Bright to help him throw a dinner party for four strangers at his Essex home. Brian McFadden hosts the game show where diners try to guess the identity of a mystery host holding a dinner party in order to win a cash prize

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The five-time Paralympian talks about her life as an elite swimmer and her journey to gold in Rio 2016

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner demonstrate dishes for a festive season table menu, including smoked salmon and seeded soda bread, chicken vol au vent, a Christmas stuffing cauliflower, and gravy carrots. Plus, a festive chocolate cake that is a twist on a Black Forest gateau

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: As the Covid-19 pandemic grips the nation, cameras follow life at Heathrow, as depleted staff members fight to keep the airport going. Customer service team member Demi is on hand to offer help to all those who are allowed to travel in and out of the airport, and has his work cut out as he tries to track down a missing passenger. Elsewhere, staff adjust to the new safety measures put in place to stop the spread of the virus

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. This time, the iconic Boeing 747 flies out of the airport for the final time, with plane spotters around the world hoping to bid it a fond farewell. Elsewhere, new cargo driver Dylan hopes his love for hypnosis can get him out of a jam

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars

A maths/shooting-led challenge rewards bold, decisive play from Billy and delivers a powerful advantage. Strategic reality quiz, hosted by Emily Atack and Danny Dyer

programme.desc: The host is joined by reality star and businesswoman Kim Kardashian and award-winning actress Sarah Paulson, who are appearing together in the new TV legal drama All's Fair. Breaking Bad star Bryan Cranston chats about taking to the West End stage in All My Sons, while actress and singer Rachel Zegler also appears, fresh from wowing audiences at the London Palladium in Evita. Plus, Little Simz performs Lion, from her album Lotus

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi tries to reverse the fortunes of Warwick Hall in Cumbria, one of her grandest hotels yet, but one suffering from low occupancy and negative feedback. While Alex is initially impressed with what she finds, she soon begins to discover the cracks and realises that she has a few harsh home truths to deliver - can she convince the owners that they need to rebrand themselves as a B&B instead of a hotel?

programme.title: Matthew Perry: A Hollywood Tragedy
programme.desc: Friends star Perry's sudden death in October 2023 shocked the world. A known addict, he was found to have ketamine in his system and months later five individuals were charged with illegally supplying him the drug. This documentary explores Perry's rise to fame and his tragic final days with insights from castmates, friends, law enforcement and Perry's own candid accounts

programme.title: Code of Silence
programme.desc: Alison Brooks, a 29-year-old deaf woman, works in the canteen of Kent and Canterbury police station, where her lip-reading skills thrust her into a high-stakes police investigation. Recruited by DI James Marsh and DS Ashleigh Francis, Alison proves invaluable as she assists in surveilling a gang planning a major heist, while she also has to navigate her mother's financial struggles, a broken relationship, and the ableist attitudes she encounters at work. Crime drama, starring Rose Ayling-Ellis, Andrew Buchan and Charlotte Ritchie

programme.title: The Six O'Clock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts another charity edition of the game show, with Guz Khan, Angela Scanlon and Joel Corry all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When a woman is involved in a hit-and-run following a local dance, Miller finds himself racing to discover the identity of the culprit. Meanwhile, David decides he has had enough of Peggy's miserly ways and walks out on the taxi service

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A prisoner is broken out of Ashfordly prison to pilot a helicopter on a secret mission, and Debbie begins acting strangely when a familiar face returns to the village. Meanwhile, Rob is left distraught when a boy takes revenge for the death of his dog

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A Frenchwoman arrives to help with preparations for Aidensfield's twinning with her home town, but things go wrong when she is left to the tender mercies of Peggy. Rob marks his return to the village by saving a con-man after his car crashes into the river, while district nurse Carol Cassidy struggles to treat a woman with an over-protective husband. Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay star

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Kersey and Lane investigate the death of a fisherman, who was killed while at sea, and find EC quotas are taking their toll in more ways than one. They also discover a new sense of rivalry in their working relationship, but a bit of healthy competition could give them the edge they need to solve the case. Jack Shepherd stars, with Jimmy Yuill and Helen Masters

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A psychiatrist's wife is murdered in a seemingly unprovoked attack, and establishing a motive proves harder than catching the killer. Meanwhile, Lane and Kersey disagree at every turn of the investigation. Jack Shepherd stars as the dour detective

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain is arrested, while Belle is worried she has overstepped the mark and Gabby is thrilled to think Ross is flirting with her

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Moira berates Cain, while tensions are running high at the garage and Ross enjoys a small victory

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Aaron is stressed as Robert's accusations over who the arsonist could be intensify, while Kammy makes a run for it and Laurel confronts Ross

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Aaron loses it, Kammy is taken away in a police car and Laurel fights her confusing attraction

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Gabby attempts to seduce Ross, but he rejects her advances, and Kerry agrees to look after Archie while Jai heads to Canada

programme.title: Soapy Slip-Ups
programme.desc: Julie Walters narrates a selection of out-takes from the programme's archives, with stars Simon Gregson, Ben Price, Alexandra Mardell, Ian Bartholomew, Jack P Shepherd, Charlie de Melo, Sue Devaney and Daniel Brocklebank fondly recalling those moments when things did not quite go to plan on the famous cobbles

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Gruff detective Jack Frost's plans for a quiet weekend are disrupted by the gruesome discovery of an unidentified body floating in the river. However, soon after he has begun the investigation, he is forced to apply his deductive skills to a separate case involving an act of violence during a football match which left a player in a coma. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Larry Lamb, Bruce Alexander, Lindy Whiteford and Berwick Kaler

programme.title: Marcus Wareing's Tales from a Kitchen Garden
programme.desc: One of the most important ingredients that Marcus uses is salt, and that is something he cannot produce himself, so he travels to Maldon in Essex to learn how it is made. Then the chef assists farmer Stuart, who needs help prepping some sheepskins that he is making into rugs

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: They Bikers explore Merseyside and The Wirral, dropping in at a forward-thinking farm, a Chinese institution, San's Café at Liverpool's docks, Chris's Chippy and The Seafood Shack in the Wirral. They cook dishes including a traditional egg foo yung, and with the pork from their farm visit they add a porky twist, plus their own take on Chris' salt and pepper chips

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Presenter Helen Skelton and expert garden designer Danny Clarke head to Bradford to breathe new life into a thoroughly overgrown garden lost in time. The aim is that they will get the job completed in just one day for owner Beverley and her family

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson meets actor-hotelier Robert Gray before assessing his three-bedroom B&B in Greenwich, south-east London - a property whose lack of signage and overgrown garden make the front door hard to find. Established to maintain cash-flow between acting jobs, the former antiques dealer's property has been filled with unusual art and objects, and is in desperate need of attention

programme.title: Karen Pirie
programme.desc: With the suspects under attack, Karen's case has become a live investigation overnight, but is this someone out for revenge, or is Rosie's killer trying to silence the truth? Thrown together with DS Phil Parhatka at the most awkward time, Karen and her team try to overcome the judgement of her superiors and find the culprit in time. Crime drama, starring Lauren Lyle

programme.title: Yesterday, Today and Tomorrow
programme.desc: A man travels back in time to fix the mistakes of the past

programme.title: Cold Feet
programme.desc: The new baby twins fill David and Karen with the joys of parenthood. Pete finds himself without a roof over his head, having overstayed his welcome at Adam and Rachel's, and resorts to moving back in with Jenny, who looks to have hit the jackpot with Internet millionaire Robert. James Nesbitt, Helen Baxendale and John Thomson star

programme.title: The Big Interview
programme.desc: Colette Fitzpatrick sits down with Ryanair CEO Michael O'Leary to discuss everything from baggage charges to his row with Elon Musk

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A recently released ex-convict who served time for the rape of a local girl becomes the target of a hate campaign - prompting Rob to point the finger of suspicion at the victim's family. The bobby is shocked when old acquaintance DS Rachel Dawson arrives in Aidensfield on the trail of an escaped prisoner, while Peggy accidentally knocks down an old friend with her car. Jonathan Kerrigan stars

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A Russian trawlerman found hiding in a local woman's shed is accused of being a spy by a Home Office official (The Office star Patrick Baladi) - but Walker and Carol discover the government worker has secrets of his own to hide. Peggy turns detective after an old note found among a dead friend's belongings raises questions about her husband's disappearance. With Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Peggy begins a frantic search for David's dog Deefer after a pair of youths steal her car from outside the Aidensfield Arms with the hound in the boot. Carol and Rob have cause for concern as Rosie is troubled by the attentions of an admirer who simply will not take no for an answer - and also has a history of violence against a former girlfriend. Jonathan Kerrigan, Gwen Taylor and Vanessa Hehir star

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Suspicion falls on a pagan sect steeped in ancient beliefs when a baby is found dead. As the detective investigates, he has to juggle family and work commitments, and finds that friendship and professionalism are pushed to the limit. Jack Shepherd stars, with Jimmy Yuill and Helen Masters

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A magistrate acquaintance of Wycliffe is found hanged and all the evidence points to suicide, but the Cornish copper remains unconvinced and pursues an unorthodox investigation, eventually forcing the forensic pathologist to reconsider his verdict. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maggie attempts to rally a despondent Will, Todd makes a change to his living arrangements, Bertie becomes a casualty of Jodie's scheming, and Betsy and Dylan compete for a job at Speed Daal

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Idris Nazir arrives in town for a family reunion with Alya, Will discovers a phone number for Megan, Sarah misses Kit while he's away, and George speculates on Danielle's motive for murder

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Daniel reaches the end of his fuse, Idris sets Brody his first task, Fiz cautions Hope against befriending Will, and a phone call brings Todd shocking news

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ryan's memory is jolted when Danielle publicly accuses Todd, Idris makes a move on Leanne, Daniel owns up to Ken, and Eva approves of Hope's positive effect on Will

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya warns Leanne about Idris, Daniel celebrates a promising sign, Ryan points Lisa towards Danielle, and one resident wrestles with their conscience after lying about their movements on the night of the murder

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: A lurcher arrives at Battersea Dogs and Cats Home with a terrible injury in need of urgent surgery. Plus, Motherland stars Anna Maxwell Martin and Diane Morgan help Paul with a cavalier King Charles spaniel, and calm down an over excited Frenchie pup

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: An armed robbery ends in murder and Frost wastes no time in arresting a known crook he saw at the scene during the getaway, convinced he is one of the men responsible. With this little mystery to solve, as well as a case of four burglaries in as many days at the same house, the dogged policeman's lot proves a busy one indeed. David Jason stars, with Billy Murray and James Hayes

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: Frances takes in an evacuee from Liverpool, who is not what she expected. Erica is forced to face the consequences of her actions, while Miriam writes letters to soldiers on the front line that cause more harm than good. Laura and Richard's secret affair is jeopardised when someone sees them together, Teresa receives devastating news, and Claire must decide whether or not to stand by Spencer

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The next generation of engineering wonders, beginning with an international team in Norway who are building the largest polar expedition vessel in the world

programme.title: Live Athletics: Diamond League
programme.desc: The Meeting International Mohammed VI d'Athlétisme de Rabat. Coverage of the third round of the series, held at Rabat Olympic Stadium in Morocco. Dutch star Femke Bol produced a fine display in the 400m hurdles here last year, winning in a meeting record time of 52.46 seconds. There were also meeting records for Botswana's Tshepiso Masalela and American Jonah Koech in the men's 800m and 1500m respectively

programme.title: A Cruel Love: The Ruth Ellis Story
programme.desc: Ruth is on trial for murder. The court will hear how she came to kill David Blakely and how a woman who seemingly had it all, was driven to shoot her lover dead. Drama, starring Lucy Boynton and Toby Jones

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: With the Meredith family reeling from Dylan's death and Sean's arrest, Lisa is charged with finding his alibi and makes a game-changing discovery. Suspicions are raised about another Morecambe resident's involvement with the twins and the police rush to find them. Abbie grows closer to newcomer Vincent, whilst Rob agrees to take on a risky dare. Frustrated by the police investigation, Sean takes matters into his own hands. Starring Morven Christie, Jonas Armstrong, Tracie Bennett and Lindsey Coulson

programme.title: Cold Blood
programme.desc: One-off crime drama, featuring the return of sinister serial killer Brian Wicklow. When a prison inmate is hanged in his cell with the murderer present, he immediately falls under suspicion. However, things are never as they appear with the wily criminal, and the police once again turn to his former cellmate Jake Osbourne to gain an insight into Wicklow's chilling mind. Starring Matthew Kelly and John Hannah

programme.title: Anthony
programme.desc: In July 2005, 18-year-old Anthony Walker was murdered by two white men in an unprovoked racist attack in a Liverpool park. Jimmy McGovern's drama imagines what life may have held for Anthony if the teenager had not been killed, with the reverse chronology seeing him realise his dreams and enjoy the life he had a right to before fate - and hate - took it all away. Toheeb Jimoh stars

programme.title: The Trial of Richard Satchwell
programme.desc: An investigation into the disappearance and murder of 44-year-old Cork woman Tina Satchwell. With exclusive interviews from her family, this documentary follows the case from Tina's sudden disappearance in March 2017, through the cold-case breakthrough six years later, to the dramatic trial that finally brought her husband, Richard Satchwell, to justice

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Armed robbers target post offices around Aidensfield and Mason finds himself working with old flame Rachel Dawson, whose career is under threat following an affair with a senior colleague. Carol is not impressed when Rachel is spotted in a clinch with Joe, but he claims it was in the line of duty and completely innocent. Peggy offers to clean for Edward Walton (played by former Likely Lads actor Rodney Bewes), raising Oscar's suspicion she is after his money, but it is Edward's wife, now in a nursing home, who turns out to have a shady past

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Donna Preston leads a group of three friends looking to win prizes by defeating Babatunde Aleshe, Snoochie Shy, Harriet Kemsley and Harry Derbridge in a series of silly games

programme.title: After the Party
programme.desc: A woman accuses her husband of a sex crime against her daughter's teenage friend and no one believes her. Five years later, when her now ex-husband returns to town, her daughter pressures her to let go of her accusations and move on. Thriller set in New Zealand, starring Robyn Malcolm and Peter Mullan

programme.title: The Chase Australia
programme.desc: Andrew O'Keefe presents the Down Under version of the quiz show in which four people must work together to beat the Chaser and win a cash prize

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Matt Gillan puts three cooks through a series of culinary challenges, including how to make poached eggs with hazelnut emulsion. Only one of the amateurs can join him in the final

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The boxer talks about his life inside and outside the ring. From growing up as a triplet to when he became the first Irishman to win an Olympic Gold medal in boxing in 1992

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Bryn Williams, Kim Woodward, Ryan Simpson and Atul Kochhar pair up with their chosen home cook partners, with Pierre Koffmann on hand to judge their final dishes

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: Scotland v Curaçao (Kick-off 1.00pm). All the action from the friendly, which comes from Hampden Park, as both teams continue their preparations for the FIFA World Cup finals. Scotland qualified for the tournament for the first time since 1998 with a play-off victory over Denmark. Curaçao reached for the finals for the first time in their history and their head coach is the 78-year-old Dick Advocaat, who managed in Scotland with Rangers between 1999 and 2001

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which Emma, Hywel, Renee, Sue and Keith race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals

programme.title: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: The show visits Walthamstow in east London, where interior designer Linda Barker provides loving mum and caring sister Vikki Ward with a home makeover. Presented by Peter Andre

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Manchester, suitors Billy, Louis and Peter suitors buy an outfit for singleton Allissia. The aspiring model then goes on a date with the person whose taste in clothes they liked best

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: Behind-the-scenes look at one of the UK's busiest transport hubs, chronicling the issues that face station staff, transport workers, emergency services, commuters and tourists

programme.title: Help Me Buy a Home
programme.desc: Buyers agent Liz O'Kane records the unfiltered realities Irish people face in the search for a home, taking a raw and honest look at the emotional rollercoaster, challenges and hard-won joys of buying a property

programme.title: Made in Korea: The K-Pop Experience
programme.desc: Blaise, Dexter, James, Olly and Reese want to improve their stamina when it comes to performing, so Shindong enrols them on a military-style fitness bootcamp with a former special services commander and presidential bodyguard. They also have to perform their first ballad, written by internationally renowned hitmaker Wayne Hector, who has penned number ones for some of the world's biggest bands

programme.title: The Regency: 10 Years On
programme.desc: Exploring the hotel attack that killed David Byrne, ignited a deadly gang war, and the policing and trials that reshaped Ireland's crime landscape

programme.title: Dark Side of Reality TV
programme.desc: The impact of America's Next Top Model on some of the aspiring models involved, focusing on those for whom the show not only ruined their careers, but scarred them for life

programme.title: The Assembly
programme.desc: Singer Jade Thirlwall faces the grilling of a lifetime from an audience of autistic and learning-disabled interviewers. Does she regret selling her soul? Were the pyramids created by aliens? Does she get trapped wind? No question or line of enquiry is off the table. Expect revelation, chaos and a lot of laughs

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Pete Wicks leads a group of three friends looking to win prizes by defeating Danny Miller, Bobby Norris, Bobby Mair and Angellica Bell - in a series of silly games

programme.title: Chasing Famous
programme.desc: An intimate and unfiltered dive into one of Generation Z's most lucrative and sought after professions

programme.title: After the Party
programme.desc: Penny's friends advise her to put the past behind her, for the sake of her relationship with her daughter Grace and grandson Walt. However, she is struggling with Phil's return and despite her best attempts to play happy families causes an argument at her Walt's birthday party. When Phil takes a teaching job, Penny tries to warn the principal about him, but is rebuffed. When she tries to dig up more about Phil's years away, he warns her to back off, or risk alienating herself from the wider family. New Zealand-set thriller, starring Robyn Malcolm and Peter Mullan

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Aiden Byrne, Stephen Terry, Mary Ann Gilchrist and Matt Gillan compete with their chosen home cook partners, with Pierre Koffmann judging their final dishes

programme.title: Week in Review
programme.desc: A look back at the main local, national and international stories of the week

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents an hour of laughs, mistakes and outtakes from the quiz that were never meant to see the light of day. With contributions from chasers Anne Hegerty, Darragh Ennis, Mark Labbett, Jenny Ryan, Paul Sinha and Shaun Wallace

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Jan Leeming is the last to take on the diners this week as they try to guess the identity of their mystery celebrity host. The foursome are hoping to make headlines and end their run on a high, but will the former BBC newsreader report back with a win or will they pose a problem for her and grab the cash? Hosted by Brian McFadden

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross delivers devastating news to Rachel - he cannot spend time with her while Emily is in New York. Meanwhile, Joey discovers Chandler's secret romance. Comedy, starring Jennifer Aniston and David Schwimmer

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Emily asks Ross to move away from his friends, while Monica and Rachel are understandably taken aback to meet a hairy man who they mistake for a yeti. American comedy, starring David Schwimmer and Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Moves In
programme.desc: Ross moves in with Chandler and Joey, but the transition does not go as smoothly as expected. Danny deals Rachel's ego a blow by introducing her to an exceptionally dull date, and Phoebe becomes disillusioned with the health inspector she is seeing

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with All the Thanksgivings
programme.desc: The pals digest a huge Thanksgiving meal and reminisce about their worst holiday experiences, including the time Chandler's father left his mother in favour of the houseboy, and the occasion in 1987 when a teenage Monica overheard Chandler calling her fat. Guest starring Morgan Fairchild

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey tries to cover up Chandler and Monica's relationship - but only succeeds in making himself look stupid. Phoebe persuades Rachel to take a literature class with her, while Ross falls out with his co-workers, convinced one of them stole his prized sandwich

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Sunderland for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford presents the general knowledge quiz in which four contestants compete over three rounds to find a single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three single women battle to win the affections of a Black Country barber Josh, hoping their style choices will bag them a date

programme.title: The Voice USA
programme.desc: A recap of the semi-final round of the competition with performances from the nine artists. With judges Kelly Clarkson, John Legend and Adam Levine

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: Germany v Finland (Kick-off 7.45pm). All the action from the friendly at Mewa Arena in Mainz. Germany are playing their first of two warm-up matches before their FIFA World Cup campaign begins with a match against Curaçao on 14 June. They last faced Finland in a 2016 friendly, when goals by Max Meyer and Mesut Ozil gave them a 2-0 home win

programme.title: Living with Lucy
programme.desc: Lucy Kennedy moves in with actor Barry Keoghan in Los Angeles, and is present when he gets a very important phone call about a role in a major new movie

programme.title: The Assembly
programme.desc: Footballer-turned-presenter Gary Lineker gets the grilling of a lifetime as he faces questions from autistic and learning-disabled interviewers, asking if he was forced out by the BBC and who the worst person he has worked with is. From the Hand of God to his first love, no question is off the table and no subject out of bounds when it comes to the Assembly's line of enquiry - expect revelations, chaos, and a lot of laughs

programme.title: The Assembly
programme.desc: Actor Michael Sheen is interviewed by a cast of 35 individuals who are neurodivergent or learning disabled. No subject is out of bounds and no question is off the table as the Good Omens star is quizzed on everything from ex-girlfriends and on-screen kisses to the OBE he returned and even his favourite motorway

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host catches up with Hollywood comedy greats Jack Black and Paul Rudd, who are appearing together in horror meta-reboot Anaconda. Also on his sofa are actress and singer Jessie Buckley, who discusses playing William Shakespeare's wife Agnes in the tragic drama Hamnet, and actress and comedian Michelle de Swarte, the writer and star of hit BBC sitcom Spent. Plus, Tom Odell performs When I Close My Eyes, from his current album A Wonderful Life (Epilogue)

programme.title: The Real Housewives of New York City
programme.desc: Tinsley Mortimer moves back to the city, Ramona fills Dorinda in on the dirt she has about Luann's fiancé Tom, and Sonja throws a Mad Hatter tea party

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: Layton learns who was responsible for the experiments on Josie and Liana, before also finding out who froze the world. Big Alice approaches New Eden but can't cross the bridge

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Omid Djalili hosts the quiz show which sees nine contestants - Sue, Joe, Elias, Gerry, Michael, Trevor, Alice, Tarryn and John - using their wits to get through to the final - with the combination of numbers they are assigned representing the size of the jackpot they each play to win
